200→          the handler method (not where defer was called);
   201→
   202→        * only the handlers that actually called ``defer()`` are called again
   203→          (that is: despite talking about “deferring an event” it is actually
   204→          the handler/event combination that is deferred); and
   205→
   206→        * any deferred events get processed before the event (or action) that
   207→          caused the current invocation of the charm.
   208→
   209→        The general desire to call ``defer()`` happens when some precondition
   210→        isn't yet met. However, care should be exercised as to whether it is
   211→        better to defer this event so that it is seen again, or whether it is
   212→        better to just wait for the event that indicates the precondition has
   213→        been met.
   214→
   215→        For example, if handling a config change requires that two config
   216→        values are changed, there's no reason to defer the first
   217→        ``config-changed`` because there will be a *second* ``config-changed``
   218→        event fired when the other config value changes.
   219→
   220→        Similarly, if two events need to occur before execution can proceed
   221→        (say event A and B), the event A handler could ``defer()`` because B
   222→        has not been seen yet. However, that leads to:
   223→
   224→        1. event A fires, calls defer()
   225→
   226→        2. event B fires, event A handler is called first, still hasn't seen B
   227→           happen, so is deferred again. Then B happens, which progresses since
   228→           it has seen A.
   229→
   230→        3. At some future time, event C happens, which also checks if A can
   231→           proceed.
   232→        """
   233→        logger.debug('Deferring %s.', self)
   234→        self.deferred = True
   235→
   236→    def snapshot(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   237→        """Return the snapshot data that should be persisted.
   238→
   239→        Subclasses must override to save any custom state.
   240→        """
   241→        return {}
   242→
   243→    def restore(self, snapshot: dict[str, Any]):
   244→        """Restore the value state from the given snapshot.
   245→
   246→        Subclasses must override to restore their custom state.
   247→        """
   248→        self.deferred = False
   249→
   250→
   251→class EventSource:
   252→    """EventSource wraps an event type with a descriptor to facilitate observing and emitting.
   253→
   254→    It is generally used as::
   255→
   256→        class SomethingHappened(ops.EventBase):
   257→            pass
   258→
   259→        class SomeObject(Object):
   260→            something_happened = ops.EventSource(SomethingHappened)
   261→
   262→    With that, instances of that type will offer the ``someobj.something_happened``
   263→    attribute which is a :class:`BoundEvent`, and may be used to emit and observe
   264→    the event.
   265→    """
   266→
   267→    def __init__(self, event_type: type[EventBase]):
   268→        if not isinstance(event_type, type) or not issubclass(event_type, EventBase):
   269→            raise RuntimeError(
   270→                f'Event requires a subclass of EventBase as an argument, got {event_type}'
   271→            )
   272→        self.event_type: type[EventBase] = event_type
   273→        self.event_kind: str | None = None
   274→        self.emitter_type: type[Object] | None = None
   275→
   276→    def __set_name__(self, emitter_type: type[Object], event_kind: str):
   277→        if self.event_kind is not None:
   278→            raise RuntimeError(
   279→                'EventSource({}) reused as {}.{} and {}.{}'.format(
   280→                    self.event_type.__name__,
   281→                    # emitter_type could still be None
   282→                    getattr(self.emitter_type, '__name__', self.emitter_type),
   283→                    self.event_kind,
   284→                    emitter_type.__name__,
   285→                    event_kind,
   286→                )
   287→            )
   288→        self.event_kind = event_kind
   289→        self.emitter_type = emitter_type
   290→
   291→    def __get__(self, emitter: Object | None, emitter_type: type[Object]) -> BoundEvent:
   292→        if emitter is None:
   293→            return self  # type: ignore
   294→        # Framework might not be available if accessed as CharmClass.on.event
   295→        # rather than charm_instance.on.event, but in that case it couldn't be
   296→        # emitted anyway, so there's no point to registering it.
   297→        framework = getattr(emitter, 'framework', None)
   298→        if framework is not None:
   299→            framework.register_type(self.event_type, emitter, self.event_kind)
   300→        return BoundEvent(emitter, self.event_type, typing.cast('str', self.event_kind))
   301→
   302→
   303→class BoundEvent:
   304→    """Event bound to an Object."""
   305→
   306→    def __repr__(self):
   307→        return (
   308→            f'<BoundEvent {self.event_type.__name__} bound to '
   309→            f'{type(self.emitter).__name__}.{self.event_kind} at {hex(id(self))}>'
   310→        )
   311→
   312→    def __init__(self, emitter: Object, event_type: type[EventBase], event_kind: str):
   313→        self.emitter = emitter
   314→        self.event_type = event_type
   315→        self.event_kind = event_kind
   316→
   317→    def emit(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any):
   318→        """Emit event to all registered observers.
   319→
   320→        The current storage state is committed before and after each observer
   321→        is notified.
   322→
   323→        Note that the emission of custom events is handled immediately. In
   324→        other words, custom events are not queued, but rather nested. For
   325→        example::
   326→
   327→            1. Main hook handler (emits custom_event_1)
   328→            2.   Custom event 1 handler (emits custom_event_2)
   329→            3.     Custom event 2 handler
   330→            4.   Resume custom event 1 handler
   331→            5. Resume main hook handler
   332→        """
   333→        framework = self.emitter.framework
   334→        key = framework._next_event_key()
   335→        event = self.event_type(Handle(self.emitter, self.event_kind, key), *args, **kwargs)
   336→        event.framework = framework
   337→        framework._emit(event)
   338→
   339→
   340→class HandleKind:
   341→    """Helper descriptor to define the Object.handle_kind field.
   342→
   343→    The handle_kind for an object defaults to its type name, but it may
   344→    be explicitly overridden if desired.
   345→    """
   346→
   347→    def __get__(self, obj: Object, obj_type: type[Object]) -> str:
   348→        kind = typing.cast('str', obj_type.__dict__.get('handle_kind'))
   349→        if kind:
   350→            return kind
   351→        return obj_type.__name__
   352→
   353→
   354→class Object:
   355→    """Initialize an Object as a new leaf in :class:`Framework`, identified by `key`.
   356→
   357→    Args:
   358→        parent: parent node in the tree.
   359→        key: unique identifier for this object.
   360→
   361→    Every object belongs to exactly one framework.
   362→
   363→    Every object has a parent, which might be a framework.
   364→
   365→    We track a "path to object," which is the path to the parent, plus the object's unique
   366→    identifier. Event handlers use this identity to track the destination of their events, and the
   367→    Framework uses this id to track persisted state between event executions.
   368→
   369→    The Framework should raise an error if it ever detects that two objects with the same id have
   370→    been created.
   371→
   372→    """
   373→
   374→    handle_kind: str = HandleKind()  # type: ignore
   375→
   376→    if TYPE_CHECKING:
   377→        # to help the type checker and IDEs:
   378→        # all these are guaranteed to be set at runtime.
   379→        @property
   380→        def on(self) -> 'ObjectEvents': ...  # noqa
   381→
   382→    def __init__(self, parent: Framework | Object, key: str | None):
   383→        self.framework: Framework = None  # type: ignore
   384→        self.handle: Handle = None  # type: ignore
   385→
   386→        kind = self.handle_kind
   387→        if isinstance(parent, Framework):
   388→            self.framework = parent
   389→            # Avoid Framework instances having a circular reference to themselves.
   390→            if self.framework is self:
   391→                self.framework = weakref.proxy(self.framework)
   392→            self.handle = Handle(None, kind, typing.cast('str', key))
   393→        else:
   394→            self.framework = parent.framework
   395→            self.handle = Handle(parent, kind, typing.cast('str', key))
   396→        self.framework._track(self)  # type: ignore
   397→
   398→        # TODO Detect conflicting handles here.
   399→
   400→    @property
   401→    def model(self) -> Model:
   402→        """Shortcut for more simple access the model."""
   403→        return self.framework.model
   404→
   405→
   406→class ObjectEvents(Object):
   407→    """Convenience type to allow defining ``.on`` attributes at class level."""
   408→
   409→    handle_kind = 'on'
   410→
   411→    def __init__(self, parent: Object | None = None, key: str | None = None):
   412→        if parent is not None:
   413→            super().__init__(parent, key)
   414→        self._cache: weakref.WeakKeyDictionary[Object, ObjectEvents] = weakref.WeakKeyDictionary()
   415→
   416→    def __get__(self, emitter: Object, emitter_type: type[Object]):
   417→        if emitter is None:
   418→            return self
   419→        instance = self._cache.get(emitter)
   420→        if instance is None:
   421→            # Same type, different instance, more data. Doing this unusual construct
   422→            # means people can subclass just this one class to have their own 'on'.
   423→            instance = self._cache[emitter] = type(self)(emitter)
   424→        return instance
   425→
   426→    @classmethod
   427→    def define_event(cls, event_kind: str, event_type: type[EventBase]):
   428→        """Define an event on this type at runtime.
   429→
   430→        Note that attempting to define the same event kind more than once will
   431→        raise an "overlaps with existing type" runtime error. Ops uses a
   432→        labeling system to track and reconstruct events between hook executions
   433→        (each time a hook runs, the Juju Agent invokes a fresh instance of ops;
   434→        there is no ops process that persists on the host between hooks).
   435→        Having duplicate Python objects creates duplicate labels. Overwriting a
   436→        previously created label means that only the latter code path will be
   437→        run when the current event, if it does get deferred, is re-emitted. This
   438→        is usually not what is desired, and is error-prone and ambiguous.
   439→
   440→        Args:
   441→            event_kind: An attribute name that will be used to access the
   442→                        event. Must be a valid Python identifier, not be a keyword
   443→                        or an existing attribute.
   444→            event_type: A type of the event to define.
   445→
   446→        Raises:
   447→            RuntimeError: if the same event is defined twice, or if ``event_kind``
   448→                is an invalid name.
   449→        """
   450→        prefix = 'unable to define an event with event_kind that '
   451→        if not event_kind.isidentifier():
   452→            raise RuntimeError(f'{prefix}is not a valid python identifier: {event_kind}')
   453→        elif keyword.iskeyword(event_kind):
   454→            raise RuntimeError(f'{prefix}is a python keyword: {event_kind}')
   455→        try:
   456→            getattr(cls, event_kind)
   457→            raise RuntimeError(
   458→                f'{prefix}overlaps with an existing type {cls} attribute: {event_kind}'
   459→            )
   460→        except AttributeError:
   461→            pass
   462→
   463→        event_descriptor = EventSource(event_type)
   464→        event_descriptor.__set_name__(cls, event_kind)
   465→        setattr(cls, event_kind, event_descriptor)
   466→
   467→    def _event_kinds(self) -> list[str]:
   468→        event_kinds: list[str] = []
   469→        # We have to iterate over the class rather than instance to allow for properties which
   470→        # might call this method (e.g., event views), leading to infinite recursion.
   471→        for attr_name, attr_value in inspect.getmembers(type(self)):
   472→            if isinstance(attr_value, EventSource):
   473→                # We actually care about the bound_event, however, since it
   474→                # provides the most info for users of this method.
   475→                event_kinds.append(attr_name)
   476→        return event_kinds
   477→
   478→    def events(self) -> dict[str, BoundEvent]:
   479→        """Return a mapping of event_kinds to bound_events for all available events."""
   480→        return {event_kind: getattr(self, event_kind) for event_kind in self._event_kinds()}
   481→
   482→    def __getitem__(self, key: str) -> PrefixedEvents:
   483→        return PrefixedEvents(self, key)
   484→
   485→    def __repr__(self):
   486→        k = type(self)
   487→        event_kinds = ', '.join(sorted(self._event_kinds()))
   488→        return f'<{k.__module__}.{k.__qualname__}: {event_kinds}>'
   489→
   490→    def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> Any:
   491→        """The existence of this method tells type checkers to allow dynamic attributes.
   492→
   493→        This allows charms to access dynamically-defined events such as
   494→        ``self.on.db_relation_joined`` without Mypy/Pyright whining.
   495→        """
   496→        return super().__getattribute__(name)
   497→
   498→
   499→class PrefixedEvents:
   500→    """Events to be found in all events using a specific prefix."""
   501→
   502→    def __init__(self, emitter: Object, key: str):
   503→        self._emitter = emitter
   504→        self._prefix = key.replace('-', '_') + '_'
   505→
   506→    def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> BoundEvent:
   507→        return getattr(self._emitter, self._prefix + name)
   508→
   509→
   510→class LifecycleEvent(EventBase):
   511→    """Events tied to the lifecycle of the Framework object.
   512→
   513→    Note: Here, "lifecycle" was poorly named and has nothing to do with the
   514→    Juju [application] lifecycle.
   515→    """
   516→
   517→    def defer(self) -> NoReturn:
   518→        """Lifecycle events are not deferrable like other events.
   519→
   520→        This is because these events are run alongside each event invocation,
   521→        so deferring would always end up simply doubling the work.
   522→
   523→        Raises:
   524→            RuntimeError: always.
   525→        """
   526→        raise RuntimeError('cannot defer lifecycle events')
   527→
   528→
   529→class PreCommitEvent(LifecycleEvent):
   530→    """Event that will be emitted first on commit."""
   531→
   532→
   533→class CommitEvent(LifecycleEvent):
   534→    """Event that will be emitted second on commit."""
   535→
   536→
   537→class FrameworkEvents(ObjectEvents):
   538→    """Manager of all framework events."""
   539→
   540→    pre_commit = EventSource(PreCommitEvent)
   541→    """Triggered before the :attr:`commit` event."""
   542→
   543→    commit = EventSource(CommitEvent)
   544→    """Triggered before event data is committed to storage."""
   545→
   546→
   547→class NoTypeError(Exception):
   548→    """No class to hold it was found when restoring an event."""
   549→
   550→    def __init__(self, handle_path: str):
   551→        self.handle_path = handle_path
   552→
   553→    def __str__(self):
   554→        return f'cannot restore {self.handle_path} since no class was registered for it'
   555→
   556→
   557→# the message to show to the user when a pdb breakpoint goes active
   558→_BREAKPOINT_WELCOME_MESSAGE = """
   559→Starting pdb to debug charm operator.
   560→Run `h` for help, `c` to continue, or `exit`/CTRL-d to abort.
   561→Future breakpoints may interrupt execution again.
   562→For more information, see:
   563→https://documentation.ubuntu.com/juju/3.6/reference/juju-cli/list-of-juju-cli-commands/debug-code/
   564→
   565→"""
   566→
   567→_event_regex = r'^(|.*/)on/[a-zA-Z_]+\[\d+\]$'
   568→
   569→
   570→class Framework(Object):
   571→    """Main interface from the Charm to the ops library's infrastructure."""
   572→
   573→    on = FrameworkEvents()  # type: ignore
   574→    """Used for :meth:`observe`-ing framework-specific events."""
   575→
   576→    # Override properties from Object so that we can set them in __init__.
   577→    model: Model = None  # type: ignore
   578→    """The :class:`Model` instance for this charm."""
   579→
   580→    meta: charm.CharmMeta = None  # type: ignore
   581→    """The charm's metadata."""
   582→
   583→    charm_dir: pathlib.Path = None  # type: ignore
   584→    """The directory where the charm is running."""
   585→
   586→    _stored: StoredStateData = None  # type: ignore
   587→
   588→    # to help the type checker and IDEs:
   589→    if TYPE_CHECKING:
   590→
   591→        @property
   592→        def on(self) -> 'FrameworkEvents': ...  # noqa
   593→
   594→    def __init__(
   595→        self,
   596→        storage: SQLiteStorage | JujuStorage,
   597→        charm_dir: str | pathlib.Path,
   598→        meta: charm.CharmMeta,
   599→        model: Model,

600→        event_name: str | None = None,
   601→        juju_debug_at: set[str] | None = None,
   602→        skip_duplicate_events: bool = True,
   603→    ):
   604→        super().__init__(self, None)
   605→
   606→        # an old, deprecated __init__ interface accepted an Optional charm_dir,
   607→        #  so we have to keep supporting it:
   608→        if charm_dir is None:
   609→            logger.warning('Framework should not be initialized with `charm_dir` set to None.')
   610→            self.charm_dir = None  # type: ignore
   611→        else:
   612→            self.charm_dir = pathlib.Path(charm_dir)
   613→
   614→        if event_name:
   615→            event_name = event_name.replace('-', '_')
   616→        self._event_name = event_name
   617→        self.meta = meta
   618→        self.model = model
   619→        self.skip_duplicate_events = skip_duplicate_events
   620→        # [(observer_path, method_name, parent_path, event_key)]
   621→        self._observers: _ObserverPath = []
   622→        # {observer_path: observing Object}
   623→        self._observer: _PathToObjectMapping = weakref.WeakValueDictionary()  # type: ignore
   624→        # {object_path: object}
   625→        self._objects: _PathToSerializableMapping = weakref.WeakValueDictionary()  # type: ignore
   626→        # {(parent_path, kind): cls}
   627→        # (parent_path, kind) is the address of _this_ object: the parent path
   628→        # plus a 'kind' string that is the name of this object.
   629→        self._type_registry: dict[_ObjectPath, type[Serializable]] = {}
   630→        self._type_known: set[type[Serializable]] = set()
   631→
   632→        if isinstance(storage, (str, pathlib.Path)):
   633→            logger.warning('deprecated: Framework now takes a Storage not a path')
   634→            storage = SQLiteStorage(storage)
   635→        # TODO(benhoyt): should probably have a Storage protocol
   636→        self._storage: SQLiteStorage = storage  # type: ignore
   637→
   638→        # We can't use the higher-level StoredState because it relies on events.
   639→        self.register_type(StoredStateData, None, StoredStateData.handle_kind)
   640→        stored_handle = Handle(None, StoredStateData.handle_kind, '_stored')
   641→        try:
   642→            self._stored = typing.cast('StoredStateData', self.load_snapshot(stored_handle))
   643→        except NoSnapshotError:
   644→            self._stored = StoredStateData(self, '_stored')
   645→            self._stored['event_count'] = 0
   646→
   647→        # Flag to indicate that we already presented the welcome message in a debugger breakpoint
   648→        self._breakpoint_welcomed: bool = False
   649→        self._juju_debug_at = juju_debug_at or set()
   650→
   651→    def set_breakpointhook(self) -> Any | None:
   652→        """Hook into ``sys.breakpointhook`` so the builtin ``breakpoint()`` works as expected.
   653→
   654→        This method is called by ``main``, and is not intended to be
   655→        called by users of the framework itself outside of perhaps
   656→        some testing scenarios.
   657→
   658→        The ``breakpoint()`` function is a Python >= 3.7 feature.
   659→
   660→        Returns:
   661→            The old value of ``sys.breakpointhook``.
   662→        """
   663→        old_breakpointhook = getattr(sys, 'breakpointhook', None)
   664→        if old_breakpointhook is not None:
   665→            # Hook into builtin breakpoint, so if Python >= 3.7, devs will be able to just do
   666→            # breakpoint()
   667→            sys.breakpointhook = self.breakpoint
   668→        return old_breakpointhook
   669→
   670→    def close(self) -> None:
   671→        """Close the underlying backends."""
   672→        self._storage.close()
   673→
   674→    def _track(self, obj: Serializable):
   675→        """Track object and ensure it is the only object created using its handle path."""
   676→        if obj is self:
   677→            # Framework objects don't track themselves
   678→            return
   679→        if obj.handle.path in self.framework._objects:
   680→            raise RuntimeError(f'two objects claiming to be {obj.handle.path} have been created')
   681→        self._objects[obj.handle.path] = obj
   682→
   683→    def _forget(self, obj: Serializable):
   684→        """Stop tracking the given object. See also _track."""
   685→        self._objects.pop(obj.handle.path, None)
   686→
   687→    def commit(self) -> None:
   688→        """Save changes to the underlying backends."""
   689→        # Give a chance for objects to persist data they want to before a commit is made.
   690→        self.on.pre_commit.emit()
   691→        # Make sure snapshots are saved by instances of StoredStateData. Any possible state
   692→        # modifications in on_commit handlers of instances of other classes will not be persisted.
   693→        self.on.commit.emit()
   694→        # Save our event count after all events have been emitted.
   695→        self.save_snapshot(self._stored)
   696→        self._storage.commit()
   697→
   698→    def register_type(
   699→        self,
   700→        cls: type[Serializable],
   701→        parent: Handle | Object | None,
   702→        kind: str | None = None,
   703→    ):
   704→        """Register a type to a handle."""
   705→        parent_path: str | None = None
   706→        if isinstance(parent, Object):
   707→            parent_path = parent.handle.path
   708→        elif isinstance(parent, Handle):
   709→            parent_path = parent.path
   710→
   711→        kind_: str = kind or cls.handle_kind
   712→        self._type_registry[parent_path, kind_] = cls
   713→        self._type_known.add(cls)
   714→
   715→    def _validate_snapshot_data(self, value: StoredStateData | EventBase, data: dict[str, Any]):
   716→        if type(value) not in self._type_known:
   717→            raise RuntimeError(
   718→                f'cannot save {type(value).__name__} values before registering that type'
   719→            )
   720→
   721→        # Use marshal as a validator, enforcing the use of simple types, as later the
   722→        # information is really pickled, which is too error-prone for future evolution of the
   723→        # stored data (e.g. if the developer stores a custom object and later changes its
   724→        # class name; when unpickling the original class will not be there and event
   725→        # data loading will fail).
   726→        try:
   727→            marshal.dumps(data)
   728→        except ValueError:
   729→            msg = 'unable to save the data for {}, it must contain only simple types: {!r}'
   730→            raise ValueError(msg.format(value.__class__.__name__, data)) from None
   731→
   732→    def save_snapshot(self, value: StoredStateData | EventBase):
   733→        """Save a persistent snapshot of the provided value."""
   734→        data = value.snapshot()
   735→        self._validate_snapshot_data(value, data)
   736→        self._storage.save_snapshot(value.handle.path, data)
   737→
   738→    def load_snapshot(self, handle: Handle) -> Serializable:
   739→        """Load a persistent snapshot."""
   740→        parent_path = None
   741→        if handle.parent:
   742→            parent_path = handle.parent.path
   743→        cls_or_none = self._type_registry.get((parent_path, handle.kind))
   744→        if not cls_or_none:
   745→            raise NoTypeError(handle.path)
   746→        cls: type[Serializable] = cls_or_none
   747→        data = self._storage.load_snapshot(handle.path)
   748→        obj = cls.__new__(cls)
   749→        obj.framework = self  # type: ignore
   750→        obj.handle = handle
   751→        obj.restore(data)
   752→        self._track(obj)
   753→        return obj
   754→
   755→    def drop_snapshot(self, handle: Handle):
   756→        """Discard a persistent snapshot."""
   757→        self._storage.drop_snapshot(handle.path)
   758→
   759→    def observe(self, bound_event: BoundEvent, observer: Callable[[Any], None]):
   760→        """Register observer to be called when bound_event is emitted.
   761→
   762→        If this is called multiple times for the same event type, the
   763→        framework calls the observers in the order they were observed.
   764→
   765→        The bound_event is generally provided as an attribute of the object that emits
   766→        the event, and is created in this style::
   767→
   768→            class SomeObject:
   769→                something_happened = Event(SomethingHappened)
   770→
   771→        That event may be observed as::
   772→
   773→            framework.observe(someobj.something_happened, self._on_something_happened)
   774→
   775→        Raises:
   776→            RuntimeError: if bound_event or observer are the wrong type.
   777→        """
   778→        if not isinstance(bound_event, BoundEvent):
   779→            raise TypeError(
   780→                f'Framework.observe requires a BoundEvent as second parameter, got {bound_event}'
   781→            )
   782→        if not isinstance(observer, types.MethodType):
   783→            raise TypeError(
   784→                f"Framework.observe requires a method as the 'observer' parameter, got {observer}"
   785→            )
   786→
   787→        event_type = bound_event.event_type
   788→        event_kind = bound_event.event_kind
   789→        emitter = bound_event.emitter
   790→
   791→        self.register_type(event_type, emitter, event_kind)
   792→
   793→        if hasattr(emitter, 'handle'):
   794→            emitter_path = emitter.handle.path
   795→        else:
   796→            raise TypeError(
   797→                f'event emitter {type(emitter).__name__} must have a "handle" attribute'
   798→            )
   799→
   800→        method_name = observer.__name__
   801→
   802→        assert isinstance(observer.__self__, Object), (
   803→            "can't register observers that aren't `Object`s"
   804→        )
   805→        observer_obj = observer.__self__
   806→
   807→        # Validate that the method has an acceptable call signature.
   808→        sig = inspect.signature(observer, follow_wrapped=False)
   809→        try:
   810→            sig.bind(EventBase(None))  # type: ignore
   811→        except TypeError as e:
   812→            raise TypeError(
   813→                f'{type(observer_obj).__name__}.{method_name} must be callable with '
   814→                "only 'self' and the 'event'"
   815→            ) from e
   816→
   817→        # TODO Prevent the exact same parameters from being registered more than once.
   818→
   819→        self._observer[observer_obj.handle.path] = observer_obj
   820→        self._observers.append((observer_obj.handle.path, method_name, emitter_path, event_kind))
   821→
   822→    def _next_event_key(self) -> str:
   823→        """Return the next event key that should be used, incrementing the internal counter."""
   824→        # Increment the count first; this means the keys will start at 1, and 0
   825→        # means no events have been emitted.
   826→        self._stored['event_count'] += 1
   827→        return str(self._stored['event_count'])
   828→
   829→    def _event_is_in_storage(
   830→        self, observer_path: str, method_name: str, event_path: str, event_data: dict[str, Any]
   831→    ) -> bool:
   832→        """Check if there is already a notice with the same snapshot in the storage."""
   833→        # Check all the notices to see if there is one that is the same other
   834→        # than the event ID.
   835→        for (
   836→            existing_event_path,
   837→            existing_observer_path,
   838→            existing_method_name,
   839→        ) in self._storage.notices():
   840→            if (
   841→                existing_observer_path != observer_path
   842→                or existing_method_name != method_name
   843→                # The notices all have paths that include [id] at the end. If one
   844→                # was somehow missing, then the split would be the empty string and
   845→                # match anyway.
   846→                or existing_event_path.split('[')[0] != event_path.split('[')[0]
   847→            ):
   848→                continue
   849→            # Check if the snapshot for this notice is the same.
   850→            try:
   851→                existing_event_data = self._storage.load_snapshot(existing_event_path)
   852→            except NoSnapshotError:
   853→                existing_event_data = {}
   854→            if event_data == existing_event_data:
   855→                return True
   856→        return False
   857→
   858→    def _emit(self, event: EventBase):
   859→        """See BoundEvent.emit for the public way to call this."""
   860→        saved = False
   861→        event_path = event.handle.path
   862→        event_kind = event.handle.kind
   863→        opentelemetry.trace.get_current_span().add_event(
   864→            event.__class__.__qualname__,
   865→            attributes={'deferred': event.deferred, 'kind': event_kind},
   866→        )
   867→        parent = event.handle.parent
   868→        assert isinstance(parent, Handle), 'event handle must have a parent'
   869→        parent_path = parent.path
   870→        this_event_data = event.snapshot()
   871→        self._validate_snapshot_data(event, this_event_data)
   872→        # TODO Track observers by (parent_path, event_kind) rather than as a list of
   873→        # all observers. Avoiding linear search through all observers for every event
   874→        for observer_path, method_name, _parent_path, _event_kind in self._observers:
   875→            if _parent_path != parent_path:
   876→                continue
   877→            if _event_kind and _event_kind != event_kind:
   878→                continue
   879→            if self.skip_duplicate_events and self._event_is_in_storage(
   880→                observer_path, method_name, event_path, this_event_data
   881→            ):
   882→                logger.info(
   883→                    'Skipping notice (%s/%s/%s) - already in the queue.',
   884→                    event_path,
   885→                    observer_path,
   886→                    method_name,
   887→                )
   888→                # Note that there may be other (event+observer) pairs that are
   889→                # not in the queue, and so may need to save the snapshot.
   890→                continue
   891→            if not saved:
   892→                # Save the event for all known observers before the first notification
   893→                # takes place, so that either everyone interested sees it, or nobody does.
   894→                self._storage.save_snapshot(event.handle.path, this_event_data)
   895→                saved = True
   896→            # Again, only commit this after all notices are saved.
   897→            self._storage.save_notice(event_path, observer_path, method_name)
   898→        if saved:
   899→            from . import tracing  # break circular import
   900→
   901→            if tracing:
   902→                # To ensure that the trace data collected during the processing of early events
   903→                # (such as install or start) are not evicted from the tracing buffer before the
   904→                # tracing relation is established, we mark any dispatch where the Juju event has an
   905→                # observer as "observed".
   906→                # This increases the priority of those trace entries within the buffer, preserving
   907→                # the initial trace data longer and helping charm developers analyze the charm's
   908→                # behavior during application startup.
   909→                tracing._mark_observed()
   910→            self._reemit(event_path)
   911→
   912→    def reemit(self) -> None:
   913→        """Reemit previously deferred events to the observers that deferred them.
   914→
   915→        Only the specific observers that have previously deferred the event will be
   916→        notified again. Observers that asked to be notified about events after it's
   917→        been first emitted won't be notified, as that would mean potentially observing
   918→        events out of order.
   919→        """
   920→        self._reemit()
   921→
   922→    @contextmanager
   923→    def _event_context(self, event_name: str):
   924→        """Handles toggling the hook-is-running state in backends.
   925→
   926→        This allows e.g. harness logic to know if it is executing within a running hook context
   927→        or not.  It sets backend._hook_is_running equal to the name of the currently running
   928→        hook (e.g. "set-leader") and reverts back to the empty string when the hook execution
   929→        is completed.
   930→
   931→        Usage:
   932→
   933→            >>> import ops
   934→            >>> from ops import testing
   935→            >>> harness = testing.Harness(ops.CharmBase)
   936→            >>> with harness._event_context('db-relation-changed'):
   937→            ...     # Harness thinks it is running an event hook.
   938→            ...     pass
   939→            >>> with harness._event_context(''):
   940→            ...     # Harness thinks it is not running an event hook.
   941→            ...     pass
   942→        """
   943→        backend: _ModelBackend | None = self.model._backend if self.model else None
   944→        if not backend:
   945→            yield  # context does nothing in this case
   946→            return
   947→
   948→        old_event_name = self._event_name
   949→        self._event_name = event_name
   950→
   951→        old_hook_is_running = backend._hook_is_running
   952→        backend._hook_is_running = event_name
   953→        yield
   954→        backend._hook_is_running = old_hook_is_running
   955→
   956→        self._event_name = old_event_name
   957→
   958→    def _reemit(self, single_event_path: str | None = None):
   959→        last_event_path = None
   960→        deferred = True
   961→        notices = tuple(self._storage.notices(single_event_path))
   962→        for i, (event_path, observer_path, method_name) in enumerate(notices):
   963→            event_handle = Handle.from_path(event_path)
   964→
   965→            if last_event_path != event_path:
   966→                if not deferred and last_event_path is not None:
   967→                    self._storage.drop_snapshot(last_event_path)
   968→                last_event_path = event_path
   969→                deferred = False
   970→
   971→            try:
   972→                event = self.load_snapshot(event_handle)
   973→            except NoTypeError:
   974→                self._storage.drop_notice(event_path, observer_path, method_name)
   975→                continue
   976→
   977→            event = typing.cast('EventBase', event)
   978→            event.deferred = False
   979→            observer = self._observer.get(observer_path)
   980→
   981→            if observer:
   982→                if single_event_path is None:
   983→                    logger.debug(
   984→                        'Re-emitting deferred event %s (%d of %d in queue).',
   985→                        event,
   986→                        i + 1,
   987→                        len(notices),
   988→                    )
   989→                elif isinstance(event, LifecycleEvent):
   990→                    # Ignore Lifecycle events: they are "private" and not interesting.
   991→                    pass
   992→                elif self._event_name and self._event_name != event.handle.kind:
   993→                    # if the event we are emitting now is not the event being
   994→                    # dispatched, and it also is not an event we have deferred,
   995→                    # and is also not a lifecycle (framework-emitted) event,
   996→                    # it must be a custom event
   997→                    logger.debug('Emitting custom event %s.', event)
   998→
   999→                custom_handler = getattr(observer, method_name, None)

1000→                if custom_handler:
  1001→                    event_is_from_juju = isinstance(event, charm.HookEvent)
  1002→                    event_is_action = isinstance(event, charm.ActionEvent)
  1003→                    if event_is_from_juju:
  1004→                        event_type = 'hook'
  1005→                    elif event_is_action:
  1006→                        event_type = 'action'
  1007→                    elif isinstance(event, LifecycleEvent):
  1008→                        event_type = 'framework'
  1009→                    else:
  1010→                        event_type = 'custom'
  1011→                    obs_class = observer.__class__.__qualname__
  1012→                    with tracer.start_as_current_span(f'{event_handle.kind}: {obs_class}') as span:
  1013→                        span.set_attribute('deferred', single_event_path is None)
  1014→                        span.set_attribute('event', repr(event))
  1015→                        span.set_attribute('event_type', event_type)
  1016→                        span.set_attribute('event_class', event.__class__.__qualname__)
  1017→                        span.set_attribute('event_name', event_handle.kind)
  1018→                        span.set_attribute('handler', f'{observer_path}.{method_name}')
  1019→                        with self._event_context(event_handle.kind):
  1020→                            if (
  1021→                                event_is_from_juju or event_is_action
  1022→                            ) and self._juju_debug_at.intersection({'all', 'hook'}):
  1023→                                # Present the welcome message and run under PDB.
  1024→                                self._show_debug_code_message()
  1025→                                pdb.runcall(custom_handler, event)
  1026→                            else:
  1027→                                # Regular call to the registered method.
  1028→                                custom_handler(event)
  1029→
  1030→            else:
  1031→                logger.warning(
  1032→                    'Reference to ops.Object at path %s has been garbage collected '
  1033→                    'between when the charm was initialised and when the event was emitted. '
  1034→                    'Make sure sure you store a reference to the observer.',
  1035→                    observer_path,
  1036→                )
  1037→
  1038→            if event.deferred:
  1039→                deferred = True
  1040→            else:
  1041→                self._storage.drop_notice(event_path, observer_path, method_name)
  1042→            # We intentionally consider this event to be dead and reload it from
  1043→            # scratch in the next path.
  1044→            self.framework._forget(event)
  1045→
  1046→        if not deferred and last_event_path is not None:
  1047→            self._storage.drop_snapshot(last_event_path)
  1048→
  1049→    def _show_debug_code_message(self):
  1050→        """Present the welcome message (only once!) when using debugger functionality."""
  1051→        if not self._breakpoint_welcomed:
  1052→            self._breakpoint_welcomed = True
  1053→            print(_BREAKPOINT_WELCOME_MESSAGE, file=sys.stderr, end='')
  1054→
  1055→    def breakpoint(self, name: str | None = None):
  1056→        """Add breakpoint, optionally named, at the place where this method is called.
  1057→
  1058→        For the breakpoint to be activated the JUJU_DEBUG_AT environment variable
  1059→        must be set to "all" or to the specific name parameter provided, if any. In every
  1060→        other situation calling this method does nothing.
  1061→
  1062→        The framework also provides a standard breakpoint named "hook", that will
  1063→        stop execution when a hook event is about to be handled.
  1064→
  1065→        For those reasons, the "all" and "hook" breakpoint names are reserved.
  1066→
  1067→        Raises:
  1068→            ValueError: if the breakpoint name is invalid.
  1069→        """
  1070→        # If given, validate the name comply with all the rules
  1071→        if name is not None:
  1072→            if not isinstance(name, str):
  1073→                raise TypeError('breakpoint names must be strings')
  1074→            if name in ('hook', 'all'):
  1075→                raise ValueError('breakpoint names "all" and "hook" are reserved')
  1076→            if not re.match(r'^[a-z0-9]([a-z0-9\-]*[a-z0-9])?$', name):
  1077→                raise ValueError('breakpoint names must look like "foo" or "foo-bar"')
  1078→
  1079→        indicated_breakpoints = self._juju_debug_at
  1080→        if not indicated_breakpoints:
  1081→            return
  1082→
  1083→        if 'all' in indicated_breakpoints or name in indicated_breakpoints:
  1084→            self._show_debug_code_message()
  1085→
  1086→            # If we call set_trace() directly it will open the debugger *here*, so indicating
  1087→            # it to use our caller's frame
  1088→            code_frame = inspect.currentframe().f_back  # type: ignore
  1089→            pdb.Pdb().set_trace(code_frame)
  1090→        else:
  1091→            logger.warning(
  1092→                'Breakpoint %r skipped (not found in the requested breakpoints: %s)',
  1093→                name,
  1094→                indicated_breakpoints,
  1095→            )
  1096→
  1097→    def remove_unreferenced_events(self) -> None:
  1098→        """Remove events from storage that are not referenced.
  1099→
  1100→        In older versions of the framework, events that had no observers would get recorded but
  1101→        never deleted. This makes a best effort to find these events and remove them from the
  1102→        database.
  1103→        """
  1104→        event_regex = re.compile(_event_regex)
  1105→        to_remove: list[str] = []
  1106→        for handle_path in self._storage.list_snapshots():
  1107→            if event_regex.match(handle_path):
  1108→                notices = self._storage.notices(handle_path)
  1109→                if next(notices, None) is None:
  1110→                    # There are no notices for this handle_path, it is valid to remove it
  1111→                    to_remove.append(handle_path)
  1112→        for handle_path in to_remove:
  1113→            self._storage.drop_snapshot(handle_path)
  1114→
  1115→
  1116→class StoredStateData(Object):
  1117→    """Manager of the stored data."""
  1118→
  1119→    def __init__(self, parent: Object, attr_name: str):
  1120→        super().__init__(parent, attr_name)
  1121→        self._cache: dict[str, Any] = {}
  1122→        self.dirty: bool = False
  1123→
  1124→    def __getitem__(self, key: str) -> Any:
  1125→        return self._cache.get(key)
  1126→
  1127→    def __setitem__(self, key: str, value: Any):
  1128→        self._cache[key] = value
  1129→        self.dirty = True
  1130→
  1131→    def __contains__(self, key: str):
  1132→        return key in self._cache
  1133→
  1134→    def snapshot(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
  1135→        """Return the current state."""
  1136→        return self._cache
  1137→
  1138→    def restore(self, snapshot: dict[str, Any]):
  1139→        """Restore current state to the given snapshot."""
  1140→        self._cache = snapshot
  1141→        self.dirty = False
  1142→
  1143→    def on_commit(self, event: EventBase) -> None:
  1144→        """Save changes to the storage backend."""
  1145→        if self.dirty:
  1146→            self.framework.save_snapshot(self)
  1147→            self.dirty = False
  1148→
  1149→
  1150→class BoundStoredState:
  1151→    """Stored state data bound to a specific Object."""
  1152→
  1153→    if TYPE_CHECKING:
  1154→        # to help the type checker and IDEs:
  1155→        @property
  1156→        def _data(self) -> StoredStateData: ...
  1157→
  1158→        @property
  1159→        def _attr_name(self) -> str: ...
  1160→
  1161→    def __init__(self, parent: Object, attr_name: str):
  1162→        parent.framework.register_type(StoredStateData, parent)
  1163→
  1164→        handle = Handle(parent, StoredStateData.handle_kind, attr_name)
  1165→        try:
  1166→            data = parent.framework.load_snapshot(handle)
  1167→        except NoSnapshotError:
  1168→            data = StoredStateData(parent, attr_name)
  1169→
  1170→        # __dict__ is used to avoid infinite recursion.
  1171→        self.__dict__['_data'] = data
  1172→        self.__dict__['_attr_name'] = attr_name
  1173→
  1174→        parent.framework.observe(parent.framework.on.commit, self._data.on_commit)
  1175→
  1176→    @typing.overload
  1177→    def __getattr__(self, key: Literal['on']) -> ObjectEvents:
  1178→        pass
  1179→
  1180→    @typing.overload
  1181→    def __getattr__(self, key: str) -> Any:
  1182→        pass
  1183→
  1184→    def __getattr__(self, key: str) -> Any:
  1185→        # "on" is the only reserved key that can't be used in the data map.
  1186→        if key == 'on':
  1187→            return self._data.on
  1188→        if key not in self._data:
  1189→            raise AttributeError(f"attribute '{key}' is not stored")
  1190→        return _wrap_stored(self._data, self._data[key])
  1191→
  1192→    def __setattr__(self, key: str, value: Any):
  1193→        if key == 'on':
  1194→            raise AttributeError("attribute 'on' is reserved and cannot be set")
  1195→
  1196→        unwrapped = _unwrap_stored(self._data, value)
  1197→
  1198→        if not isinstance(unwrapped, (type(None), int, float, str, bytes, list, dict, set)):
  1199→            raise AttributeError(
  1200→                f'attribute {key!r} cannot be a {type(unwrapped).__name__}: '
  1201→                'must be int/float/dict/list/etc'
  1202→            )
  1203→
  1204→        self._data[key] = unwrapped
  1205→
  1206→    def set_default(self, **kwargs: Any):
  1207→        """Set the value of any given key if it has not already been set."""
  1208→        for k, v in kwargs.items():
  1209→            if k not in self._data:
  1210→                self._data[k] = v
  1211→

  1213→class StoredState:
  1214→    """A class used to store data the charm needs, persisted across invocations.
  1215→
  1216→    Instances of ``MyClass`` can transparently save state between invocations by
  1217→    setting attributes on ``_stored``. Initial state should be set with
  1218→    ``set_default`` on the bound object; for example::
  1219→
  1220→        class MyClass(ops.Object):
  1221→            _stored = ops.StoredState()
  1222→
  1223→            def __init__(self, parent, key):
  1224→                super().__init__(parent, key)
  1225→                self._stored.set_default(seen=set())
  1226→                self.framework.observe(self.on.seen, self._on_seen)
  1227→
  1228→            def _on_seen(self, event):
  1229→                self._stored.seen.add(event.uuid)
  1230→
  1231→    Data is stored alongside the charm (in the charm container for Kubernetes
  1232→    sidecar charms, and on the machine for machine charms). The exceptions are
  1233→    two deprecated cases: Kubernetes podspec charms, and charms explicitly
  1234→    passing ``True`` for ``use_juju_for_storage`` when running :meth:`ops.main`.
  1235→
  1236→    For machine charms, charms are upgraded in-place on the machine, so the data
  1237→    is preserved. For Kubernetes sidecar charms, when the charm is upgraded, the
  1238→    pod is replaced, so any data is lost. When data should be preserved across
  1239→    upgrades, Kubernetes sidecar charms should use a peer-relation for the data
  1240→    instead of `StoredState`.
  1241→    """
  1242→
  1243→    def __init__(self):
  1244→        self.parent_type: type[Any] | None = None
  1245→        self.attr_name: str | None = None
  1246→
  1247→    @typing.overload
  1248→    def __get__(self, parent: Literal[None], parent_type: type[_ObjectType]) -> StoredState:
  1249→        pass
  1250→
  1251→    @typing.overload
  1252→    def __get__(self, parent: _ObjectType, parent_type: type[_ObjectType]) -> BoundStoredState:
  1253→        pass
  1254→
  1255→    def __get__(
  1256→        self, parent: _ObjectType | None, parent_type: type[_ObjectType]
  1257→    ) -> StoredState | BoundStoredState:
  1258→        if self.parent_type is not None and self.parent_type not in parent_type.mro():
  1259→            # the StoredState instance is being shared between two unrelated classes
  1260→            # -> unclear what is expected of us -> bail out
  1261→            raise RuntimeError(
  1262→                f'StoredState shared by {self.parent_type.__name__} and {parent_type.__name__}'
  1263→            )
  1264→
  1265→        if parent is None:
  1266→            # accessing via the class directly (e.g. MyClass.stored)
  1267→            return self
  1268→
  1269→        bound = None
  1270→        if self.attr_name is not None:
  1271→            bound = parent.__dict__.get(self.attr_name)
  1272→            if bound is not None:
  1273→                # we already have the thing from a previous pass, huzzah
  1274→                return bound
  1275→
  1276→        # need to find ourselves amongst the parent's bases
  1277→        for cls in parent_type.mro():
  1278→            for attr_name, attr_value in cls.__dict__.items():
  1279→                if attr_value is not self:
  1280→                    continue
  1281→                # we've found ourselves! is it the first time?
  1282→                if bound is not None:
  1283→                    # the StoredState instance is being stored in two different
  1284→                    # attributes -> unclear what is expected of us -> bail out
  1285→                    raise RuntimeError(
  1286→                        f'StoredState shared by {cls.__name__}.{self.attr_name} and '
  1287→                        f'{cls.__name__}.{attr_name}'
  1288→                    )
  1289→                # we've found ourselves for the first time; save where, and bind the object
  1290→                self.attr_name = attr_name
  1291→                self.parent_type = cls
  1292→                bound = BoundStoredState(parent, attr_name)
  1293→
  1294→        if bound is not None:
  1295→            # cache the bound object to avoid the expensive lookup the next time
  1296→            # (don't use setattr, to keep things symmetric with the fast-path lookup above)
  1297→
  1298→            # attr_name is optional at descriptor level, but we're bound now: it's
  1299→            # guaranteed to be a string. We need to help the type checker:
  1300→            assert isinstance(self.attr_name, str)
  1301→            parent.__dict__[self.attr_name] = bound
  1302→            return bound
  1303→
  1304→        raise AttributeError(
  1305→            f'cannot find {self.__class__.__name__} attribute in type {parent_type.__name__}'
  1306→        )
  1307→
  1308→
  1309→def _wrap_stored(parent_data: StoredStateData, value: Any) -> Any:
  1310→    if isinstance(value, dict):
  1311→        return StoredDict(parent_data, value)  # type: ignore
  1312→    if isinstance(value, list):
  1313→        return StoredList(parent_data, value)  # type: ignore
  1314→    if isinstance(value, set):
  1315→        return StoredSet(parent_data, value)  # type: ignore
  1316→    return value
  1317→
  1318→
  1319→def _unwrap_stored(parent_data: StoredStateData, value: Any) -> Any:
  1320→    if isinstance(value, (StoredDict, StoredList, StoredSet)):
  1321→        return value._under
  1322→    return value
  1323→
  1324→
  1325→def _wrapped_repr(obj: _StoredObject) -> str:
  1326→    t = type(obj)
  1327→    if obj._under:
  1328→        return f'{t.__module__}.{t.__name__}({obj._under!r})'
  1329→    else:
  1330→        return f'{t.__module__}.{t.__name__}()'

  1333→class StoredDict(typing.MutableMapping[Hashable, Any]):
  1334→    """A dict-like object that uses the StoredState as backend.
  1335→
  1336→    Charms are not expected to use this class directly. Adding a
  1337→    :class:`StoredState` attribute to a charm class will automatically use this
  1338→    class to store dictionaries.
  1339→    """
  1340→
  1341→    def __init__(self, stored_data: StoredStateData, under: dict[Hashable, Any]):
  1342→        self._stored_data = stored_data
  1343→        self._under = under
  1344→
  1345→    def __getitem__(self, key: Hashable):
  1346→        return _wrap_stored(self._stored_data, self._under[key])
  1347→
  1348→    def __setitem__(self, key: Hashable, value: Any):
  1349→        self._under[key] = _unwrap_stored(self._stored_data, value)
  1350→        self._stored_data.dirty = True
  1351→
  1352→    def __delitem__(self, key: Hashable):
  1353→        del self._under[key]
  1354→        self._stored_data.dirty = True
  1355→
  1356→    def __iter__(self):
  1357→        return self._under.__iter__()
  1358→
  1359→    def __len__(self):
  1360→        return len(self._under)
  1361→
  1362→    def __eq__(self, other: Any):
  1363→        if isinstance(other, StoredDict):
  1364→            return self._under == other._under
  1365→        elif isinstance(other, collections.abc.Mapping):
  1366→            return self._under == other
  1367→        else:
  1368→            return NotImplemented
  1369→
  1370→    __repr__ = _wrapped_repr  # type: ignore

  1373→class StoredList(typing.MutableSequence[Any]):

  1375→

  1377→    :class:`StoredState` attribute to a charm class will automatically use this
  1378→    class to store lists.

  1381→    def __init__(self, stored_data: StoredStateData, under: list[Any]):
  1382→        self._stored_data = stored_data
  1383→        self._under = under
  1384→

  1386→        return _wrap_stored(self._stored_data, self._under[index])
  1387→
  1388→    def __setitem__(self, index: int, value: Any):
  1389→        self._under[index] = _unwrap_stored(self._stored_data, value)
  1390→        self._stored_data.dirty = True

  1392→    def __delitem__(self, index: int):

  1399→    def insert(self, index: int, value: Any):
  1400→        """Insert value before index."""
  1401→        self._under.insert(index, value)
  1402→        self._stored_data.dirty = True

  1404→    def append(self, value: Any):

  1409→    def __eq__(self, other: Any):

  1412→        elif isinstance(other, list):

  1414→        else:
  1415→            return NotImplemented
  1416→
  1417→    def __lt__(self, other: Any):
  1418→        if isinstance(other, StoredList):
  1419→            return self._under < other._under
  1420→        elif isinstance(other, list):
  1421→            return self._under < other
  1422→        else:
  1423→            return NotImplemented
  1424→
  1425→    def __le__(self, other: Any):
  1426→        if isinstance(other, StoredList):
  1427→            return self._under <= other._under
  1428→        elif isinstance(other, list):
  1429→            return self._under <= other
  1430→        else:
  1431→            return NotImplemented
  1432→
  1433→    def __gt__(self, other: Any):
  1434→        if isinstance(other, StoredList):
  1435→            return self._under > other._under
  1436→        elif isinstance(other, list):
  1437→            return self._under > other
  1438→        else:
  1439→            return NotImplemented
  1440→
  1441→    def __ge__(self, other: Any):
  1442→        if isinstance(other, StoredList):

  1444→        elif isinstance(other, list):
  1445→            return self._under >= other
  1446→        else:
  1447→            return NotImplemented
  1448→
  1449→    __repr__ = _wrapped_repr  # type: ignore

  1452→class StoredSet(typing.MutableSet[Any]):
  1453→    """A set-like object that uses the StoredState as backend.
  1454→
  1455→    Charms are not expected to use this class directly. Adding a
  1456→    :class:`StoredState` attribute to a charm class will automatically use this
  1457→    class to store sets.
  1458→    """
  1459→
  1460→    def __init__(self, stored_data: StoredStateData, under: set[Any]):
  1461→        self._stored_data = stored_data
  1462→        self._under = under
  1463→
  1464→    def add(self, key: Any):
  1465→        """Add a key to a set.
  1466→
  1467→        This has no effect if the key is already present.
  1468→        """
  1469→        self._under.add(key)
  1470→        self._stored_data.dirty = True
  1471→
  1472→    def discard(self, key: Any):
  1473→        """Remove a key from a set if it is a member.
  1474→
  1475→        If the key is not a member, do nothing.
  1476→        """
  1477→        self._under.discard(key)
  1478→        self._stored_data.dirty = True
  1479→
  1480→    def __contains__(self, key: Any):
  1481→        return key in self._under
  1482→
  1483→    def __iter__(self):
  1484→        return self._under.__iter__()
  1485→
  1486→    def __len__(self):
  1487→        return len(self._under)
  1488→
  1489→    @classmethod
  1490→    def _from_iterable(cls, it: Iterable[_T]) -> set[_T]:
  1491→        """Construct an instance of the class from any iterable input.
  1492→
  1493→        Per https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.abc.html
  1494→        if the Set mixin is being used in a class with a different constructor signature,
  1495→        override _from_iterable() with a classmethod that can construct new instances
  1496→        from an iterable argument.
  1497→        """
  1498→        return set(it)
  1499→
  1500→    def __le__(self, other: Any):
  1501→        if isinstance(other, StoredSet):
  1502→            return self._under <= other._under
  1503→        elif isinstance(other, collections.abc.Set):
  1504→            return self._under <= other
  1505→        else:
  1506→            return NotImplemented
  1507→
  1508→    def __ge__(self, other: Any):
  1509→        if isinstance(other, StoredSet):
  1510→            return self._under >= other._under
  1511→        elif isinstance(other, collections.abc.Set):
  1512→            return self._under >= other
  1513→        else:
  1514→            return NotImplemented
  1515→
  1516→    def __eq__(self, other: Any):
  1517→        if isinstance(other, StoredSet):
  1518→            return self._under == other._under
  1519→        elif isinstance(other, collections.abc.Set):
  1520→            return self._under == other
  1521→        else:
  1522→            return NotImplemented

200→        - ReturnCode

   217→        Args:

   222→    def fail(self, message: str = ''):
   223→        """Report that this action has failed.

   230→    def load_params(
   231→        self,
   232→        cls: type[_T],
   233→        *args: Any,
   234→        errors: Literal['raise', 'fail'] = 'raise',
   235→        **kwargs: Any,
   236→    ) -> _T:
   237→        """Load the action parameters into an instance of an action class.
   238→
   239→        The raw Juju action parameters are passed to the action class's
   240→        ``__init__`` method as keyword arguments, with dashes in names
   241→        converted to underscores.
   242→
   243→        For dataclasses and Pydantic ``BaseModel`` subclasses, only fields in
   244→        the Juju action parameters that have a matching field in the class are
   245→        passed as arguments.
   246→
   247→        For example::
   248→
   249→            class BackupParams(pydantic.BaseModel):
   250→                filename: str
   251→
   252→            def _on_do_backup(self, event: ops.ActionEvent):
   253→                params = event.load_params(BackupParams)
   254→                # params.filename contains the value passed by the Juju user.
   255→
   256→        Any additional positional or keyword arguments will be passed through to
   257→        the action class ``__init__``.
   258→
   259→        Args:
   260→            cls: A class that will accept the Juju parameters as keyword
   261→                arguments, and raise ``ValueError`` if validation fails.
   262→            errors: what to do if the parameters are invalid. If ``fail``, this
   263→                will set the action to failed with an appropriate message and
   264→                then immediately exit. If ``raise``, ``load_params`` will not
   265→                catch any exceptions, leaving the charm to handle errors.
   266→            args: positional arguments to pass through to the action class.
   267→            kwargs: keyword arguments to pass through to the action class.
   268→
   269→        Returns:
   270→            An instance of the action class that was provided in the ``cls``
   271→            argument with the provided parameter values.
   272→
   273→        Raises:
   274→            ValueError: if ``errors`` is set to ``raise`` and instantiating the
   275→                action class raises a ValueError.
   276→        """
   277→        try:
   278→            fields = _juju_fields(cls)
   279→        except ValueError:
   280→            fields = None
   281→        params: dict[str, Any] = kwargs.copy()
   282→        for key, value in sorted(self.params.items()):
   283→            attr = key.replace('-', '_')
   284→            if fields is None:
   285→                params[attr] = value
   286→            else:
   287→                if attr not in fields:
   288→                    continue
   289→                params[fields[attr]] = value
   290→        try:
   291→            return cls(*args, **params)
   292→        except ValueError as e:
   293→            if errors == 'raise':
   294→                raise
   295→            self.fail(f'Error in action parameters: {e}')
   296→            from ._main import _Abort
   297→
   298→            raise _Abort(0) from e
   299→
   300→    def __repr__(self):
   301→        return f'<{self.__class__.__name__} {self.id=} via {self.handle}>'

   316→class StartEvent(HookEvent):
   317→    """Event triggered immediately after first configuration change.
   318→
   319→    This event is triggered immediately after the first
   320→    :class:`~ops.ConfigChangedEvent`. Callback methods bound to the event should be
   321→    used to ensure that the charm's software is in a running state. Note that
   322→    the charm's software should be configured so as to persist in this state
   323→    through reboots without further intervention on Juju's part.
   324→
   325→    See also: `Juju | Hook | start <https://documentation.ubuntu.com/juju/3.6/reference/hook/#start>`_.
   326→    """

   329→class StopEvent(HookEvent):
   330→    """Event triggered when a charm is shut down.
   331→
   332→    This event is triggered when an application's removal is requested
   333→    by the client. The event fires immediately before the end of the
   334→    unit's destruction sequence. Callback methods bound to this event
   335→    should be used to ensure that the charm's software is not running,
   336→    and that it will not start again on reboot.
   337→
   338→    See also: `Juju | Hook | stop <https://documentation.ubuntu.com/juju/3.6/reference/hook/#stop>`_.
   339→    """
   340→
   341→    def defer(self) -> NoReturn:
   342→        """Stop events are not deferrable like other events.
   343→
   344→        This is because the unit is in the process of tearing down, and there
   345→        will not be an opportunity for the deferred event to run.
   346→
   347→        Raises:
   348→            RuntimeError: always.
   349→        """
   350→        raise RuntimeError('cannot defer stop events')

   370→        raise RuntimeError('cannot defer remove events')

   373→class ConfigChangedEvent(HookEvent):
   374→    """Event triggered when a configuration change occurs.
   375→
   376→    This event will fire in several situations:
   377→
   378→    - When the admin reconfigures the charm using the Juju CLI, for example
   379→      ``juju config mycharm foo=bar``. This event notifies the charm of
   380→      its new configuration. (The event itself, however, is not aware of *what*
   381→      specifically has changed in the config).
   382→    - Right after the unit starts up for the first time.
   383→      This event notifies the charm of its initial configuration.
   384→      Typically, this event will fire between an :class:`~ops.InstallEvent`
   385→      and a :class:`~ops.StartEvent` during the startup sequence
   386→      (when a unit is first deployed), but in general it will fire whenever
   387→      the unit is (re)started, for example after pod churn on Kubernetes, on unit
   388→      rescheduling, on unit upgrade or refresh, and so on.
   389→    - As a specific instance of the above point: when networking changes
   390→      (if the machine reboots and comes up with a different IP).
   391→    - When the app config changes, for example when `juju trust` is run.
   392→
   393→    Any callback method bound to this event cannot assume that the
   394→    software has already been started; it should not start stopped
   395→    software, but should (if appropriate) restart running software to
   396→    take configuration changes into account.
   397→
   398→    See also: `Juju | Hook | config-changed <https://documentation.ubuntu.com/juju/3.6/reference/hook/#config-changed>`_.
   399→    """
   400→

   415→    See also: `Juju | Hook | update-status <https://documentation.ubuntu.com/juju/3.6/reference/hook/#update-status>`_.

   434→class PreSeriesUpgradeEvent(HookEvent):
   435→    """Event triggered to prepare a unit for series upgrade.
   436→
   437→    This event triggers when an administrator executes ``juju upgrade-machine
   438→    <machine> prepare``. The event will fire for each unit that is running on the
   439→    specified machine. Any callback method bound to this event must prepare the
   440→    charm for an upgrade to the series. This may include things like exporting
   441→    database content to a version neutral format, or evacuating running
   442→    instances to other machines.
   443→
   444→    It can be assumed that only after all units on a machine have executed the
   445→    callback method associated with this event, the administrator will initiate
   446→    steps to actually upgrade the series.  After the upgrade has been completed,
   447→    the :class:`~ops.PostSeriesUpgradeEvent` will fire.
   448→
   449→    See also: `Juju | Hook | pre-series-upgrade <https://documentation.ubuntu.com/juju/3.6/reference/hook/#pre-series-upgrade>`_.

   452→    """

   457→            DeprecationWarning,
   458→            stacklevel=3,
   459→        )
   460→        super().__init__(handle)
