   553→class RelationEvent(HookEvent):
   554→    """A base class representing the various relation lifecycle events.
   555→
   556→    Relation lifecycle events are generated when application units
   557→    participate in relations.  Units can only participate in relations
   558→    after they have been "started", and before they have been
   559→    "stopped". Within that time window, the unit may participate in
   560→    several different relations at a time, including multiple
   561→    relations with the same name.
   562→    """
   563→
   564→    relation: model.Relation
   565→    """The relation involved in this event."""
   566→
   567→    app: model.Application
   568→    """The remote application that has triggered this event."""
   569→
   570→    unit: model.Unit | None
   571→    """The remote unit that has triggered this event.
   572→
   573→    This will be ``None`` if the relation event was triggered as an
   574→    :class:`Application <model.Application>`-level event.
   575→    """
   576→
   577→    def __init__(
   578→        self,
   579→        handle: Handle,
   580→        relation: model.Relation,
   581→        app: model.Application | None = None,
   582→        unit: model.Unit | None = None,
   583→    ):
   584→        super().__init__(handle)
   585→
   586→        if unit is not None and unit.app != app:
   587→            raise RuntimeError(
   588→                f'cannot create RelationEvent with application {app} and unit {unit}'
   589→            )
   590→
   591→        self.relation = relation
   592→        if app is None:
   593→            logger.warning(
   594→                "'app' expected but not received, see https://bugs.launchpad.net/juju/+bug/1960934"
   595→            )
   596→            # Do an explicit assignment here so that we can contain the type: ignore.
   597→            self.app = None  # type: ignore
   598→        else:
   599→            self.app = app

600→        self.unit = unit
   601→
   602→    def snapshot(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   603→        """Used by the framework to serialize the event to disk.
   604→
   605→        Not meant to be called by charm code.
   606→        """
   607→        snapshot: dict[str, Any] = {
   608→            'relation_name': self.relation.name,
   609→            'relation_id': self.relation.id,
   610→        }
   611→        if self.app:
   612→            snapshot['app_name'] = self.app.name
   613→        if self.unit:
   614→            snapshot['unit_name'] = self.unit.name
   615→        return snapshot
   616→
   617→    def restore(self, snapshot: dict[str, Any]):
   618→        """Used by the framework to deserialize the event from disk.
   619→
   620→        Not meant to be called by charm code.
   621→        """
   622→        relation = self.framework.model.get_relation(
   623→            snapshot['relation_name'], snapshot['relation_id']
   624→        )
   625→        if relation is None:
   626→            raise ValueError(
   627→                'Unable to restore {}: relation {} (id={}) not found.'.format(
   628→                    self, snapshot['relation_name'], snapshot['relation_id']
   629→                )
   630→            )
   631→        self.relation = relation
   632→
   633→        app_name = snapshot.get('app_name')
   634→        if app_name:
   635→            self.app = self.framework.model.get_app(app_name)
   636→        else:
   637→            logger.warning("'app_name' expected in snapshot but not found.")
   638→            self.app = None  # type: ignore
   639→
   640→        unit_name = snapshot.get('unit_name')
   641→        if unit_name:
   642→            self.unit = self.framework.model.get_unit(unit_name)
   643→        else:
   644→            self.unit = None
   645→
   646→    def __repr__(self):
   647→        app = None if self.app is None else self.app.name
   648→        unit = None if self.unit is None else self.unit.name
   649→        return f'<{self.__class__.__name__} {app=} {unit=} on {self.relation!r} via {self.handle}>'
   650→
   651→
   652→class RelationCreatedEvent(RelationEvent):
   653→    """Event triggered when a new relation is created.
   654→
   655→    This is triggered when a new relation with another app is added in Juju. This
   656→    can occur before units for those applications have started. All existing
   657→    relations will trigger `RelationCreatedEvent` before :class:`~ops.StartEvent` is
   658→    emitted.
   659→
   660→    See also: `Juju | Hook | <endpoint>-relation-created <https://documentation.ubuntu.com/juju/3.6/reference/hook/#endpoint-relation-created>`_.
   661→    """
   662→
   663→    unit: None  # pyright: ignore[reportIncompatibleVariableOverride]
   664→    """Always ``None``."""
   665→
   666→
   667→class RelationJoinedEvent(RelationEvent):
   668→    """Event triggered when a new unit joins a relation.
   669→
   670→    This event is triggered whenever a new unit of an integrated
   671→    application joins the relation.  The event fires only when that
   672→    remote unit is first observed by the unit. Callback methods bound
   673→    to this event may set any local unit data that can be
   674→    determined using no more than the name of the joining unit and the
   675→    remote ``private-address`` setting, which is always available when
   676→    the relation is created and is by convention not deleted.
   677→
   678→    See also: `Juju | Hook | <endpoint>-relation-joined <https://documentation.ubuntu.com/juju/3.6/reference/hook/#endpoint-relation-joined>`_.
   679→    """
   680→
   681→    unit: model.Unit  # pyright: ignore[reportIncompatibleVariableOverride]
   682→    """The remote unit that has triggered this event."""
   683→
   684→
   685→class RelationChangedEvent(RelationEvent):
   686→    """Event triggered when relation data changes.
   687→
   688→    This event is triggered whenever there is a change to the data bucket for an
   689→    integrated application or unit. Look at ``event.relation.data[event.unit/app]``
   690→    to see the new information, where ``event`` is the event object passed to
   691→    the callback method bound to this event.
   692→
   693→    This event always fires once, after :class:`~ops.RelationJoinedEvent`, and
   694→    will subsequently fire whenever that remote unit changes its data for
   695→    the relation. Callback methods bound to this event should be the only ones
   696→    that rely on remote relation data. They should not error if the data
   697→    is incomplete, since it can be guaranteed that when the remote unit or
   698→    application changes its data, the event will fire again.
   699→
   700→    The data that may be queried, or set, are determined by the relation's
   701→    interface.
   702→
   703→    See also: `Juju | Hook | <endpoint>-relation-changed <https://documentation.ubuntu.com/juju/3.6/reference/hook/#endpoint-relation-changed>`_.
   704→    """
   705→
   706→
   707→class RelationDepartedEvent(RelationEvent):
   708→    """Event triggered when a unit leaves a relation.
   709→
   710→    This is the inverse of the :class:`~ops.RelationJoinedEvent`, representing when a
   711→    unit is leaving the relation (the unit is being removed, the app is being
   712→    removed, the relation is being removed). For remaining units, this event is
   713→    emitted once for each departing unit.  For departing units, this event is
   714→    emitted once for each remaining unit.
   715→
   716→    Callback methods bound to this event may be used to remove all
   717→    references to the departing remote unit, because there's no
   718→    guarantee that it's still part of the system; it's perfectly
   719→    probable (although not guaranteed) that the system running that
   720→    unit has already shut down.
   721→
   722→    Once all callback methods bound to this event have been run for such a
   723→    relation, the unit agent will fire the :class:`~ops.RelationBrokenEvent`.
   724→
   725→    See also: `Juju | Hook | <endpoint>-relation-departed <https://documentation.ubuntu.com/juju/3.6/reference/hook/#endpoint-relation-departed>`_.
   726→    """
   727→
   728→    unit: model.Unit  # pyright: ignore[reportIncompatibleVariableOverride]
   729→    """The remote unit that has triggered this event."""
   730→
   731→    def __init__(
   732→        self,
   733→        handle: Handle,
   734→        relation: model.Relation,
   735→        app: model.Application | None = None,
   736→        unit: model.Unit | None = None,
   737→        departing_unit_name: str | None = None,
   738→    ):
   739→        super().__init__(handle, relation, app=app, unit=unit)
   740→
   741→        self._departing_unit_name = departing_unit_name
   742→
   743→    def snapshot(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   744→        """Used by the framework to serialize the event to disk.
   745→
   746→        Not meant to be called by charm code.
   747→        """
   748→        snapshot = super().snapshot()
   749→        if self._departing_unit_name:
   750→            snapshot['departing_unit'] = self._departing_unit_name
   751→        return snapshot
   752→
   753→    @property
   754→    def departing_unit(self) -> model.Unit | None:
   755→        """The :class:`ops.Unit` that is departing, if any.
   756→
   757→        Use this method to determine (for example) whether this unit is the
   758→        departing one.
   759→        """
   760→        # doing this on init would fail because `framework` gets patched in
   761→        # post-init
   762→        if not self._departing_unit_name:
   763→            return None
   764→        return self.framework.model.get_unit(self._departing_unit_name)
   765→
   766→    def restore(self, snapshot: dict[str, Any]):
   767→        """Used by the framework to deserialize the event from disk.
   768→
   769→        Not meant to be called by charm code.
   770→        """
   771→        super().restore(snapshot)
   772→        self._departing_unit_name = snapshot.get('departing_unit')
   773→
   774→
   775→class RelationBrokenEvent(RelationEvent):
   776→    """Event triggered when a relation is removed.
   777→
   778→    If a relation is being removed (``juju remove-relation`` or ``juju
   779→    remove-application``), once all the units have been removed, this event will
   780→    fire to signal that the relationship has been fully terminated.
   781→
   782→    The event indicates that the current relation is no longer valid, and that
   783→    the charm's software must be configured as though the relation had never
   784→    existed. It will only be called after every callback method bound to
   785→    :class:`~ops.RelationDepartedEvent` has been run. If a callback method
   786→    bound to this event is being executed, it is guaranteed that no remote units
   787→    are currently known locally.
   788→
   789→    See also: `Juju | Hook | <endpoint>-relation-broken <https://documentation.ubuntu.com/juju/3.6/reference/hook/#endpoint-relation-broken>`_.
   790→    """
   791→
   792→    unit: None  # pyright: ignore[reportIncompatibleVariableOverride]
   793→    """Always ``None``."""
   794→
   795→
   796→class StorageEvent(HookEvent):
   797→    """Base class representing events to do with storage.
   798→
   799→    Juju can provide a variety of storage types to a charms. The
   800→    charms can define several different types of storage that are
   801→    allocated from Juju. Changes in state of storage trigger sub-types
   802→    of :class:`StorageEvent`.
   803→    """
   804→
   805→    storage: model.Storage
   806→    """Storage instance this event refers to."""
   807→
   808→    def __init__(self, handle: Handle, storage: model.Storage):
   809→        super().__init__(handle)
   810→        self.storage = storage
   811→
   812→    def snapshot(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   813→        """Used by the framework to serialize the event to disk.
   814→
   815→        Not meant to be called by charm code.
   816→        """
   817→        snapshot: dict[str, Any] = {}
   818→        if isinstance(self.storage, model.Storage):
   819→            snapshot['storage_name'] = self.storage.name
   820→            snapshot['storage_index'] = self.storage.index
   821→            snapshot['storage_location'] = str(self.storage.location)
   822→        return snapshot
   823→
   824→    def restore(self, snapshot: dict[str, Any]):
   825→        """Used by the framework to deserialize the event from disk.
   826→
   827→        Not meant to be called by charm code.
   828→        """
   829→        storage_name = snapshot.get('storage_name')
   830→        storage_index = snapshot.get('storage_index')
   831→        storage_location = snapshot.get('storage_location')
   832→
   833→        if storage_name and storage_index is not None:
   834→            storages = self.framework.model.storages[storage_name]
   835→            self.storage = next((s for s in storages if s.index == storage_index), None)  # type: ignore
   836→            if self.storage is None:
   837→                raise RuntimeError(
   838→                    f'failed loading storage (name={storage_name!r}, '
   839→                    f'index={storage_index!r}) from snapshot'
   840→                )
   841→            if storage_location is None:
   842→                raise RuntimeError(
   843→                    'failed loading storage location from snapshot.'
   844→                    f'(name={storage_name!r}, index={storage_index!r}, storage_location=None)'
   845→                )
   846→
   847→            self.storage.location = storage_location
   848→
   849→    def __repr__(self):
   850→        return f'<{self.__class__.__name__} on {self.storage!r} via {self.handle}>'
   851→
   852→
   853→class StorageAttachedEvent(StorageEvent):
   854→    """Event triggered when new storage becomes available.
   855→
   856→    This event is triggered when new storage is available for the
   857→    charm to use.
   858→
   859→    Callback methods bound to this event allow the charm to run code
   860→    when storage has been added. Such methods will be run before the
   861→    :class:`~ops.InstallEvent` fires, so that the installation routine may
   862→    use the storage. The name prefix of this hook will depend on the
   863→    storage key defined in the ``metadata.yaml`` file.
   864→
   865→    See also: `Juju | Hook | <storage>-storage-attached <https://documentation.ubuntu.com/juju/3.6/reference/hook/#storage-storage-attached>`_.
   866→    """
   867→
   868→
   869→class StorageDetachingEvent(StorageEvent):
   870→    """Event triggered prior to removal of storage.
   871→
   872→    This event is triggered when storage a charm has been using is
   873→    going away.
   874→
   875→    Callback methods bound to this event allow the charm to run code
   876→    before storage is removed. Such methods will be run before storage
   877→    is detached, and always before the :class:`~ops.StopEvent` fires, thereby
   878→    allowing the charm to gracefully release resources before they are
   879→    removed and before the unit terminates. The name prefix of the
   880→    hook will depend on the storage key defined in the ``metadata.yaml``
   881→    file.
   882→
   883→    See also: `Juju | Hook | <storage>-storage-detaching <https://documentation.ubuntu.com/juju/3.6/reference/hook/#storage-storage-detaching>`_.
   884→    """

   887→class WorkloadEvent(HookEvent):
   888→    """Base class representing events to do with the workload.

   890→    Workload events are generated for all containers that the charm
   891→    expects in metadata.
   892→    """
   893→
   894→    workload: model.Container
   895→    """The workload involved in this event.
   896→
   897→    Workload currently only can be a :class:`Container <model.Container>`, but
   898→    in future may be other types that represent the specific workload type,
   899→    for example a machine.
   900→    """
   901→
   902→    def __init__(self, handle: Handle, workload: model.Container):
   903→        super().__init__(handle)
   904→
   905→        self.workload = workload
   906→
   907→    def snapshot(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   908→        """Used by the framework to serialize the event to disk.
   909→
   910→        Not meant to be called by charm code.
   911→        """
   912→        snapshot: dict[str, Any] = {}
   913→        if isinstance(self.workload, model.Container):
   914→            snapshot['container_name'] = self.workload.name
   915→        return snapshot
   916→
   917→    def restore(self, snapshot: dict[str, Any]):
   918→        """Used by the framework to deserialize the event from disk.
   919→
   920→        Not meant to be called by charm code.
   921→        """
   922→        container_name = snapshot.get('container_name')
   923→        if container_name:
   924→            self.workload = self.framework.model.unit.get_container(container_name)
   925→        else:
   926→            self.workload = None  # type: ignore
   927→
   928→    def __repr__(self):
   929→        return f'<{self.__class__.__name__} on {self.workload!r} via {self.handle}>'

   932→class PebbleReadyEvent(WorkloadEvent):

   935→    This event is triggered when the Pebble process for a workload/container
   936→    starts up, allowing the charm to configure how services should be launched.
   937→
   938→    Callback methods bound to this event allow the charm to run code after
   939→    a workload has started its Pebble instance and is ready to receive instructions
   940→    regarding what services should be started. The name prefix of the hook
   941→    will depend on the container key defined in the ``metadata.yaml`` file.

   947→class PebbleNoticeEvent(WorkloadEvent):
   948→    """Base class for Pebble notice events (each notice type is a subclass)."""
   949→
   950→    notice: model.LazyNotice
   951→    """Provide access to the event notice's details."""
   952→
   953→    def __init__(
   954→        self,
   955→        handle: Handle,
   956→        workload: model.Container,
   957→        notice_id: str,
   958→        notice_type: str,
   959→        notice_key: str,
   960→    ):
   961→        super().__init__(handle, workload)
   962→        self.notice = model.LazyNotice(workload, notice_id, notice_type, notice_key)
   963→
   964→    def snapshot(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   965→        """Used by the framework to serialize the event to disk.
   966→
   967→        Not meant to be called by charm code.
   968→        """
   969→        d = super().snapshot()
   970→        d['notice_id'] = self.notice.id
   971→        d['notice_type'] = (
   972→            self.notice.type if isinstance(self.notice.type, str) else self.notice.type.value
   973→        )
   974→        d['notice_key'] = self.notice.key
   975→        return d
   976→
   977→    def restore(self, snapshot: dict[str, Any]):
   978→        """Used by the framework to deserialize the event from disk.
   979→
   980→        Not meant to be called by charm code.
   981→        """
   982→        super().restore(snapshot)
   983→        notice_id = snapshot.pop('notice_id')
   984→        notice_type = snapshot.pop('notice_type')
   985→        notice_key = snapshot.pop('notice_key')
   986→        self.notice = model.LazyNotice(self.workload, notice_id, notice_type, notice_key)

200→
   201→    For example 'secret:dcc7aa9c-7202-4da6-8d5f-0fbbaa4e1a41' (from ``JUJU_SECRET_ID``).

   235→
   236→    @classmethod
   237→    def _from_dict(cls, env: Mapping[str, Any]) -> JujuContext:
   238→        return JujuContext(
   239→            action_name=env.get('JUJU_ACTION_NAME') or None,
   240→            action_uuid=env.get('JUJU_ACTION_UUID') or None,
   241→            availability_zone=env.get('JUJU_AVAILABILITY_ZONE') or None,
   242→            # If JUJU_CHARM_DIR is None or set to an empty string, use Path(f'{__file__}/../../..')
   243→            # as default (assuming the '$JUJU_CHARM_DIR/lib/op/main.py' structure).
   244→            charm_dir=(
   245→                Path(env['JUJU_CHARM_DIR']).resolve()
   246→                if env.get('JUJU_CHARM_DIR')
   247→                else Path(f'{__file__}/../../..').resolve()
   248→            ),
   249→            debug='JUJU_DEBUG' in env,
   250→            debug_at=(
   251→                {x.strip() for x in env['JUJU_DEBUG_AT'].split(',')}
   252→                if env.get('JUJU_DEBUG_AT')
   253→                else set()
   254→            ),
   255→            dispatch_path=env.get('JUJU_DISPATCH_PATH', ''),
   256→            hook_name=env.get('JUJU_HOOK_NAME', ''),
   257→            machine_id=env.get('JUJU_MACHINE_ID') or None,
   258→            model_name=env.get('JUJU_MODEL_NAME', ''),
   259→            model_uuid=env.get('JUJU_MODEL_UUID', ''),
   260→            notice_id=env.get('JUJU_NOTICE_ID') or None,
   261→            notice_key=env.get('JUJU_NOTICE_KEY') or None,
   262→            notice_type=env.get('JUJU_NOTICE_TYPE') or None,
   263→            pebble_check_name=env.get('JUJU_PEBBLE_CHECK_NAME') or None,
   264→            principal_unit=env.get('JUJU_PRINCIPAL_UNIT') or None,
   265→            relation_departing_unit_name=env.get('JUJU_DEPARTING_UNIT') or None,
   266→            relation_name=env.get('JUJU_RELATION') or None,
   267→            relation_id=(
   268→                int(env['JUJU_RELATION_ID'].split(':')[-1])
   269→                if env.get('JUJU_RELATION_ID')
   270→                else None
   271→            ),
   272→            remote_app_name=env.get('JUJU_REMOTE_APP') or None,
   273→            remote_unit_name=env.get('JUJU_REMOTE_UNIT') or None,
   274→            secret_id=env.get('JUJU_SECRET_ID') or None,
   275→            secret_label=env.get('JUJU_SECRET_LABEL') or None,
   276→            secret_revision=(
   277→                int(env['JUJU_SECRET_REVISION']) if env.get('JUJU_SECRET_REVISION') else None
   278→            ),
   279→            storage_index=(
   280→                int(env['JUJU_STORAGE_ID'].split('/')[-1]) if env.get('JUJU_STORAGE_ID') else None
   281→            ),
   282→            storage_name=(
   283→                env.get('JUJU_STORAGE_ID', '').split('/')[0]
   284→                if env.get('JUJU_STORAGE_ID')
   285→                else None
   286→            ),
   287→            unit_name=env.get('JUJU_UNIT_NAME', ''),
   288→            # The meter-status-changed event, triggered by `juju set-meter-status`,
   289→            # does not set JUJU_VERSION, but all other events do. When we drop support
   290→            # for Juju 2 and Juju 3 we can change this to always expect JUJU_VERSION,
   291→            # as that event no longer exists in Juju 4.
   292→            version=JujuVersion(env.get('JUJU_VERSION', '0.0.0')),
   293→            workload_name=env.get('JUJU_WORKLOAD_NAME') or None,
   294→        )
   295→
   296→    @classmethod
   297→    def from_environ(cls, environ: Mapping[str, str] | None = None) -> JujuContext:
   298→        """Create a ``JujuContext`` object from the environment.
   299→
   300→        If environ is ``None``, ``os.environ`` will be used.
   301→
   302→        Raises:
   303→            ValueError: If any required environment variables are missing.
   304→        """
   305→        if environ is None:
   306→            environ = os.environ
   307→        assert environ is not None
   308→
   309→        required = (
   310→            'JUJU_DISPATCH_PATH',
   311→            'JUJU_HOOK_NAME',
   312→            'JUJU_MODEL_NAME',
   313→            'JUJU_MODEL_UUID',
   314→            'JUJU_UNIT_NAME',
   315→            'JUJU_VERSION',
   316→        )
   317→        for var in required:
   318→            if var not in environ:
   319→                raise ValueError(f'Missing required environment variable: {var}')
   320→
   321→        event = environ['JUJU_HOOK_NAME']
   322→        # These events have no additional context.
   323→        if event in (
   324→            'install',
   325→            'start',
   326→            'stop',
   327→            'remove',
   328→            'config-changed',
   329→            'update-status',
   330→            'upgrade-charm',
   331→            'leader-elected',
   332→        ):
   333→            return cls._from_dict(environ)
   334→
   335→        if event in ('secret-changed', 'secret-rotate', 'secret-remove', 'secret-expired'):
   336→            # JUJU_SECRET_LABEL may be missing if the secret does not have a label.
   337→            if 'JUJU_SECRET_ID' not in environ:
   338→                raise ValueError('Missing required environment variable: JUJU_SECRET_ID')
   339→            if (
   340→                event in ('secret-remove', 'secret-expired')
   341→                and 'JUJU_SECRET_REVISION' not in environ
   342→            ):
   343→                raise ValueError('Missing required environment variable: JUJU_SECRET_REVISION')
   344→
   345→        if event == '' and environ['JUJU_DISPATCH_PATH'].startswith('actions/'):
   346→            for var in ('JUJU_ACTION_NAME', 'JUJU_ACTION_UUID'):
   347→                if var not in environ:
   348→                    raise ValueError(f'Missing required environment variable: {var}')
   349→
   350→        if event.endswith('-pebble-custom-notice'):
   351→            for var in (
   352→                'JUJU_WORKLOAD_NAME',
   353→                'JUJU_NOTICE_ID',
   354→                'JUJU_NOTICE_KEY',
   355→                'JUJU_NOTICE_TYPE',
   356→            ):
   357→                if var not in environ:
   358→                    raise ValueError(f'Missing required environment variable: {var}')
   359→
   360→        if event.endswith(('-pebble-check-failed', '-pebble-check-recovered')):
   361→            for var in ('JUJU_WORKLOAD_NAME', 'JUJU_PEBBLE_CHECK_NAME'):
   362→                if var not in environ:
   363→                    raise ValueError(f'Missing required environment variable: {var}')
   364→
   365→        if event.endswith('-pebble-ready') and 'JUJU_WORKLOAD_NAME' not in environ:
   366→            raise ValueError('Missing required environment variable: JUJU_WORKLOAD_NAME')
   367→
   368→        if (
   369→            event.endswith(('-storage-attached', '-storage-detaching'))
   370→            and 'JUJU_STORAGE_ID' not in environ
   371→        ):
   372→            raise ValueError('Missing required environment variable: JUJU_STORAGE_ID')
   373→
   374→        if event.endswith((
   375→            '-relation-created',
   376→            '-relation-joined',
   377→            '-relation-changed',
   378→            '-relation-departed',
   379→            '-relation-broken',
   380→        )):
   381→            for var in ('JUJU_RELATION', 'JUJU_RELATION_ID', 'JUJU_REMOTE_APP'):
   382→                if var not in environ:
   383→                    raise ValueError(f'Missing required environment variable: {var}')
   384→            if (
   385→                event.endswith(('-relation-joined', '-relation-changed', '-relation-departed'))
   386→                and 'JUJU_REMOTE_UNIT' not in environ
   387→            ):
   388→                raise ValueError('Missing required environment variable: JUJU_REMOTE_UNIT')
   389→            if event.endswith('-relation-departed') and 'JUJU_DEPARTING_UNIT' not in environ:
   390→                raise ValueError('Missing required environment variable: JUJU_DEPARTING_UNIT')
   391→
   392→        return cls._from_dict(environ)
   393→

  1032→class PebbleCheckFailedEvent(PebbleCheckEvent):

  1060→class SecretEvent(HookEvent):

  1066→        self._label = label

  1068→    @property

  1070→        """The secret instance this event refers to.
  1071→

  1073→        until :meth:`Secret.get_content()` is called.
  1074→        """
  1075→        backend = self.framework.model._backend
  1076→        return model.Secret(
  1077→            backend=backend,
  1078→            id=self._id,
  1079→            label=self._label,
  1080→        )
  1081→
  1082→    def snapshot(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
  1083→        """Used by the framework to serialize the event to disk.
  1084→
  1085→        Not meant to be called by charm code.
  1086→        """
  1087→        return {'id': self._id, 'label': self._label}

  1089→    def restore(self, snapshot: dict[str, Any]):
  1090→        """Used by the framework to deserialize the event from disk.
  1091→
  1092→        Not meant to be called by charm code.
  1093→        """
  1094→        self._id = cast('str', snapshot['id'])
  1095→        self._label = cast('str | None', snapshot['label'])

  1098→class SecretChangedEvent(SecretEvent):
  1099→    """Event triggered on the secret observer charm when the secret owner changes its contents.
  1100→
  1101→    When the owner of a secret changes the secret's contents, Juju will create
  1102→    a new secret revision, and all applications or units that are tracking this
  1103→    secret will be notified via this event that a new revision is available.
  1104→
  1105→    Typically, the charm will fetch the new content by calling
  1106→    :meth:`event.secret.get_content() <ops.Secret.get_content>` with ``refresh=True``
  1107→    to tell Juju to start tracking the new revision.
  1108→
  1109→    See also: `Juju | Hook | secret-changed <https://documentation.ubuntu.com/juju/3.6/reference/hook/#secret-changed>`_.
  1110→
  1111→    .. jujuadded:: 3.0
  1112→        Charm secrets added in Juju 3.0, user secrets added in Juju 3.3
  1113→    """
  1114→
  1115→
  1116→class SecretRotateEvent(SecretEvent):
  1117→    """Event triggered on the secret owner charm when the secret's rotation policy elapses.
  1118→
  1119→    This event is fired on the secret owner to inform it that the secret must
  1120→    be rotated. The event will keep firing until the owner creates a new
  1121→    revision by calling :meth:`event.secret.set_content() <ops.Secret.set_content>`.
  1122→
  1123→    See also: `Juju | Hook | secret-rotate <https://documentation.ubuntu.com/juju/3.6/reference/hook/#secret-rotate>`_.
  1124→
  1125→    .. jujuadded:: 3.0
  1126→    """
  1127→
  1128→    def defer(self) -> NoReturn:
  1129→        """Secret rotation events are not deferrable (Juju handles re-invocation).
  1130→
  1131→        Raises:
  1132→            RuntimeError: always.
  1133→        """
  1134→        raise RuntimeError(
  1135→            'Cannot defer secret rotation events. Juju will keep firing this '
  1136→            'event until you create a new revision.'
  1137→        )
  1138→

  1140→class SecretRemoveEvent(SecretEvent):
  1141→    """Event triggered on the secret owner charm when a secret revision can be removed.
  1142→
  1143→    When the owner of a secret creates a new revision, and after all
  1144→    observers have updated to that new revision, this event will be fired to
  1145→    inform the secret owner that the old revision can be removed.
  1146→
  1147→    After any required cleanup, the charm should call
  1148→    :meth:`event.remove_revision() <ops.SecretRemoveEvent.remove_revision>` to
  1149→    remove the now-unused revision. If the charm does not, then the event will
  1150→    be emitted again, when further revisions are ready for removal.
  1151→
  1152→    See also: `Juju | Hook | secret-remove <https://documentation.ubuntu.com/juju/3.6/reference/hook/#secret-remove>`_.
  1153→
  1154→    .. jujuadded:: 3.0
  1155→    """
  1156→
  1157→    def __init__(self, handle: Handle, id: str, label: str | None, revision: int):
  1158→        super().__init__(handle, id, label)
  1159→        self._revision = revision
  1160→
  1161→    @property
  1162→    def revision(self) -> int:
  1163→        """The secret revision this event refers to."""
  1164→        return self._revision
  1165→
  1166→    def remove_revision(self):
  1167→        """Remove the revision this event refers to.
  1168→
  1169→        Call this method after any required cleanup to inform Juju that the
  1170→        secret revision can be removed.
  1171→        """
  1172→        self.secret.remove_revision(self._revision)
  1173→
  1174→    def snapshot(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
  1175→        """Used by the framework to serialize the event to disk.
  1176→
  1177→        Not meant to be called by charm code.
  1178→        """
  1179→        data = super().snapshot()
  1180→        data['revision'] = self._revision
  1181→        return data
  1182→
  1183→    def restore(self, snapshot: dict[str, Any]):
  1184→        """Used by the framework to deserialize the event from disk.
  1185→
  1186→        Not meant to be called by charm code.
  1187→        """
  1188→        super().restore(snapshot)
  1189→        self._revision = cast('int', snapshot['revision'])
  1190→

  1222→    def snapshot(self) -> dict[str, Any]:

  1251→class CollectStatusEvent(LifecycleEvent):
  1252→    """Event triggered at the end of every hook to collect statuses for evaluation.
  1253→
  1254→    If the charm wants to provide application or unit status in a consistent
  1255→    way after the end of every hook, it should observe the
  1256→    :attr:`collect_app_status <CharmEvents.collect_app_status>` or
  1257→    :attr:`collect_unit_status <CharmEvents.collect_unit_status>` event,
  1258→    respectively.
  1259→
  1260→    The framework will trigger these events after the hook code runs
  1261→    successfully (``collect_app_status`` will only be triggered on the leader
  1262→    unit). This happens on every Juju event, whether it was
  1263→    :meth:`observed <ops.Framework.observe>` or not.
  1264→    If any statuses were added by the event handler using
  1265→    :meth:`add_status`, the framework will choose the highest-priority status
  1266→    and set that as the status (application status for ``collect_app_status``,
  1267→    or unit status for ``collect_unit_status``).
  1268→
  1269→    The order of priorities is as follows, from highest to lowest:
  1270→
  1271→    * blocked
  1272→    * maintenance
  1273→    * waiting
  1274→    * active
  1275→
  1276→    It is an error to call :meth:`add_status` with an instance of
  1277→    :class:`ErrorStatus` or :class:`UnknownStatus`.
  1278→
  1279→    If there are multiple statuses with the same priority, the first one added
  1280→    wins (and if an event is observed multiple times, the handlers are called
  1281→    in the order they were observed).
  1282→
  1283→    A collect-status event can be observed multiple times, and
  1284→    :meth:`add_status` can be called multiple times to add multiple statuses
  1285→    for evaluation. This is useful when a charm has multiple components that
  1286→    each have a status. Each code path in a collect-status handler should
  1287→    call ``add_status`` at least once.
  1288→
  1289→    Below is an example "web app" charm component that observes
  1290→    ``collect_unit_status`` to provide the status of the component, which
  1291→    requires a "port" config option set before it can proceed::
  1292→
  1293→        class MyCharm(ops.CharmBase):
  1294→            def __init__(self, framework: ops.Framework):
  1295→                super().__init__(framework)
  1296→                self.webapp = Webapp(self)
  1297→                # initialize other components
  1298→
  1299→        class WebApp(ops.Object):
  1300→            def __init__(self, charm: ops.CharmBase):
  1301→                super().__init__(charm, 'webapp')
  1302→                self.framework.observe(charm.on.collect_unit_status, self._on_collect_status)
  1303→
  1304→            def _on_collect_status(self, event: ops.CollectStatusEvent):
  1305→                if 'port' not in self.model.config:
  1306→                    event.add_status(ops.BlockedStatus('please set "port" config'))
  1307→                    return
  1308→                event.add_status(ops.ActiveStatus())
  1309→    """  # noqa: D405, D214, D411, D416  Final return confuses docstyle.
  1310→
  1311→    def add_status(self, status: model.StatusBase):
  1312→        """Add a status for evaluation.
  1313→
  1314→        See :class:`CollectStatusEvent` for a description of how to use this.
  1315→        """
  1316→        if not isinstance(status, model.StatusBase):
  1317→            raise TypeError(f'status should be a StatusBase, not {type(status).__name__}')
  1318→        if status.name not in model._SETTABLE_STATUS_NAMES:
  1319→            raise model.InvalidStatusError(
  1320→                f'status.name must be in {model._SETTABLE_STATUS_NAMES}, not {status.name!r}'
  1321→            )
  1322→        model_ = self.framework.model
  1323→        if self.handle.kind == 'collect_app_status':
  1324→            if not isinstance(status, model.ActiveStatus):
  1325→                logger.debug('Adding app status %s', status, stacklevel=2)
  1326→            model_.app._collected_statuses.append(status)
  1327→        else:
  1328→            if not isinstance(status, model.ActiveStatus):
  1329→                logger.debug('Adding unit status %s', status, stacklevel=2)
  1330→            model_.unit._collected_statuses.append(status)
  1331→
  1332→
  1333→class CharmEvents(ObjectEvents):
  1334→    """Events generated by Juju pertaining to application lifecycle.
  1335→
  1336→    By default, the events listed as attributes of this class will be
  1337→    provided via the :attr:`CharmBase.on` attribute. For example::
  1338→
  1339→        framework.observe(self.on.config_changed, self._on_config_changed)
  1340→
  1341→    In addition to the events listed as attributes of this class,
  1342→    dynamically-named events will also be defined based on the charm's
  1343→    metadata (``metadata.yaml``) for relations, storage, actions, and
  1344→    containers. These named events may be accessed as
  1345→    ``self.on[<name>].<event>`` or using a prefix like
  1346→    ``self.on.<name>_<event>``, for example::
  1347→
  1348→        framework.observe(self.on["db"].relation_created, self._on_db_relation_created)
  1349→        framework.observe(self.on.workload_pebble_ready, self._on_workload_pebble_ready)
  1350→    """
  1351→
  1352→    # NOTE: The one-line docstrings below are copied from the first line of
  1353→    #       each event class's docstring. Please keep in sync.
  1354→
  1355→    install = EventSource(InstallEvent)
  1356→    """Triggered when a charm is installed (see :class:`~ops.InstallEvent`)."""
  1357→
  1358→    start = EventSource(StartEvent)
  1359→    """Triggered immediately after first configuration change (see :class:`~ops.StartEvent`)."""
  1360→
  1361→    stop = EventSource(StopEvent)
  1362→    """Triggered when a charm is shut down (see :class:`~ops.StopEvent`)."""
  1363→
  1364→    remove = EventSource(RemoveEvent)
  1365→    """Triggered when a unit is about to be terminated (see :class:`~ops.RemoveEvent`)."""
  1366→
  1367→    update_status = EventSource(UpdateStatusEvent)
  1368→    """Triggered periodically by a status update request from Juju (see
  1369→    :class:`~ops.UpdateStatusEvent`).
  1370→    """
  1371→
  1372→    config_changed = EventSource(ConfigChangedEvent)
  1373→    """Triggered when a configuration change occurs (see :class:`~ops.ConfigChangedEvent`)."""
  1374→
  1375→    upgrade_charm = EventSource(UpgradeCharmEvent)
  1376→    """Triggered by request to upgrade the charm (see :class:`~ops.UpgradeCharmEvent`)."""
  1377→
  1378→    pre_series_upgrade = EventSource(PreSeriesUpgradeEvent)
  1379→    """Triggered to prepare a unit for series upgrade (see :class:`~ops.PreSeriesUpgradeEvent`).
  1380→
  1381→    .. jujuremoved:: 4.0
  1382→    """
  1383→
  1384→    post_series_upgrade = EventSource(PostSeriesUpgradeEvent)
  1385→    """Triggered after a series upgrade (see :class:`~ops.PostSeriesUpgradeEvent`).
  1386→
  1387→    .. jujuremoved:: 4.0
  1388→    """
  1389→
  1390→    leader_elected = EventSource(LeaderElectedEvent)
  1391→    """Triggered when a new leader has been elected (see :class:`~ops.LeaderElectedEvent`)."""
  1392→
  1393→    leader_settings_changed = EventSource(LeaderSettingsChangedEvent)
  1394→    """Triggered when leader changes any settings (see
  1395→    :class:`~ops.LeaderSettingsChangedEvent`).
  1396→
  1397→    .. deprecated:: 2.4.0
  1398→    """
  1399→
  1400→    collect_metrics = EventSource(CollectMetricsEvent)
  1401→    """Triggered by Juju to collect metrics (see :class:`~ops.CollectMetricsEvent`).
  1402→
  1403→    .. jujuremoved:: 3.6.11
  1404→    """
  1405→
  1406→    secret_changed = EventSource(SecretChangedEvent)
  1407→    """Triggered by Juju on the observer when the secret owner changes its contents (see
  1408→    :class:`~ops.SecretChangedEvent`).
  1409→
  1410→    .. jujuadded:: 3.0
  1411→        Charm secrets added in Juju 3.0, user secrets added in Juju 3.3
  1412→    """
  1413→
  1414→    secret_expired = EventSource(SecretExpiredEvent)
  1415→    """Triggered by Juju on the owner when a secret's expiration time elapses (see
  1416→    :class:`~ops.SecretExpiredEvent`).
  1417→
  1418→    .. jujuadded:: 3.0
  1419→    """
  1420→
  1421→    secret_rotate = EventSource(SecretRotateEvent)
  1422→    """Triggered by Juju on the owner when the secret's rotation policy elapses (see
  1423→    :class:`~ops.SecretRotateEvent`).
  1424→
  1425→    .. jujuadded:: 3.0
  1426→    """
  1427→
  1428→    secret_remove = EventSource(SecretRemoveEvent)
  1429→    """Triggered by Juju on the owner when a secret revision can be removed (see

  1432→    .. jujuadded:: 3.0

  1434→
  1435→    collect_app_status = EventSource(CollectStatusEvent)

  1449→class CharmBase(Object):
  1450→    """Base class that represents the charm overall.
  1451→
  1452→    :code:`CharmBase` is used to create a charm. This is done by inheriting
  1453→    from :code:`CharmBase` and customising the subclass as required. So to
  1454→    create a charm called ``MyCharm``, define a charm class and set up the
  1455→    required event handlers (“hooks”) in its constructor::
  1456→
  1457→        import logging
  1458→
  1459→        import ops
  1460→
  1461→        class MyCharm(ops.CharmBase):
  1462→            def __init__(self, framework: ops.Framework):
  1463→                super().__init__(framework)
  1464→                framework.observe(self.on.config_changed, self._on_config_changed)
  1465→                framework.observe(self.on.stop, self._on_stop)
  1466→                # ...
  1467→
  1468→        if __name__ == "__main__":
  1469→            ops.main(MyCharm)
  1470→
  1471→    As shown in the example above, a charm class is instantiated by
  1472→    :code:`ops.main` rather than charm authors directly instantiating a
  1473→    charm.
  1474→
  1475→    Args:
  1476→        framework: The framework responsible for managing the Model and events for this
  1477→            charm.
  1478→    """
  1479→
  1480→    on: CharmEvents = CharmEvents()  # type: ignore
  1481→    """This property is used to create an event handler using :meth:`Framework.observe`,
  1482→    and can be one of the events listed at :class:`CharmEvents`.
  1483→    """
  1484→
  1485→    if TYPE_CHECKING:
  1486→        # to help the type checker and IDEs:
  1487→        @property
  1488→        def on(self) -> CharmEvents: ...  # noqa
  1489→
  1490→    def __init__(self, framework: Framework):
  1491→        super().__init__(framework, None)
  1492→
  1493→        for relation_name in self.framework.meta.relations:
  1494→            relation_name = relation_name.replace('-', '_')
  1495→            self.on.define_event(f'{relation_name}_relation_created', RelationCreatedEvent)
  1496→            self.on.define_event(f'{relation_name}_relation_joined', RelationJoinedEvent)
  1497→            self.on.define_event(f'{relation_name}_relation_changed', RelationChangedEvent)
  1498→            self.on.define_event(f'{relation_name}_relation_departed', RelationDepartedEvent)
  1499→            self.on.define_event(f'{relation_name}_relation_broken', RelationBrokenEvent)

1500→
  1501→        for storage_name in self.framework.meta.storages:
  1502→            storage_name = storage_name.replace('-', '_')
  1503→            self.on.define_event(f'{storage_name}_storage_attached', StorageAttachedEvent)
  1504→            self.on.define_event(f'{storage_name}_storage_detaching', StorageDetachingEvent)
  1505→
  1506→        for action_name in self.framework.meta.actions:
  1507→            action_name = action_name.replace('-', '_')
  1508→            self.on.define_event(f'{action_name}_action', ActionEvent)
  1509→
  1510→        for container_name in self.framework.meta.containers:
  1511→            container_name = container_name.replace('-', '_')
  1512→            self.on.define_event(f'{container_name}_pebble_ready', PebbleReadyEvent)
  1513→            self.on.define_event(f'{container_name}_pebble_custom_notice', PebbleCustomNoticeEvent)
  1514→            self.on.define_event(f'{container_name}_pebble_check_failed', PebbleCheckFailedEvent)
  1515→            self.on.define_event(
  1516→                f'{container_name}_pebble_check_recovered', PebbleCheckRecoveredEvent
  1517→            )
  1518→
  1519→    @property
  1520→    def app(self) -> model.Application:
  1521→        """The application that this unit is part of."""
  1522→        return self.framework.model.app
  1523→
  1524→    @property
  1525→    def unit(self) -> model.Unit:
  1526→        """The current unit."""
  1527→        return self.framework.model.unit
  1528→
  1529→    @property
  1530→    def meta(self) -> CharmMeta:
  1531→        """Metadata of this charm."""
  1532→        return self.framework.meta
  1533→
  1534→    @property
  1535→    def charm_dir(self) -> pathlib.Path:
  1536→        """Root directory of the charm as it is running."""
  1537→        return self.framework.charm_dir
  1538→
  1539→    @property
  1540→    def config(self) -> model.ConfigData:
  1541→        """A mapping containing the charm's config and current values."""
  1542→        return self.model.config
  1543→
  1544→    def load_config(
  1545→        self,
  1546→        cls: type[_T],
  1547→        *args: Any,
  1548→        errors: Literal['raise', 'blocked'] = 'raise',
  1549→        **kwargs: Any,
  1550→    ) -> _T:
  1551→        """Load the config into an instance of a config class.
  1552→
  1553→        The raw Juju config is passed to the config class's ``__init__``, as
  1554→        keyword arguments, with the following changes:
  1555→
  1556→        * ``secret`` type options have a :class:`model.Secret` value rather
  1557→          than the secret ID. Note that the secret object is not validated by
  1558→          Juju at this time, so may raise :class:`SecretNotFoundError` when it
  1559→          is used later (if the secret does not exist or the unit does not have
  1560→          permission to access it).
  1561→        * dashes in names are converted to underscores.
  1562→
  1563→        For dataclasses and Pydantic ``BaseModel`` subclasses, only fields in
  1564→        the Juju config that have a matching field in the class are passed as
  1565→        arguments. Pydantic fields that have an ``alias``, or dataclasses that
  1566→        have a ``metadata{'alias'=}``, will have the alias applied when loading.
  1567→
  1568→        For example::
  1569→
  1570→            class Config(pydantic.BaseModel):
  1571→                # This field is called 'class' in the Juju config options.
  1572→                workload_class: str = pydantic.Field(alias='class')
  1573→
  1574→            def _on_config_changed(self, event: ops.ConfigChangedEvent):
  1575→                data = self.load_config(Config, errors='blocked')
  1576→                # `data.workload_class` has the value of the Juju option `class`
  1577→
  1578→        Pydantic classes that have fields that are not simple or
  1579→        Pydantic types, such as :class:`ops.Secret`, require setting
  1580→        ``arbitrary_types_allowed`` in the Pydantic model config.
  1581→
  1582→        Any additional positional or keyword arguments to this method will be
  1583→        passed through to the config class ``__init__``.
  1584→
  1585→        Args:
  1586→            cls: A class that will accept the Juju options as keyword arguments,
  1587→                and raise ``ValueError`` if validation fails.
  1588→            errors: what to do if the config is invalid. If ``blocked``, this
  1589→                will set the unit status to blocked with an appropriate message
  1590→                and then exit successfully (this informs Juju that
  1591→                the event was handled and it will not be retried).
  1592→                If ``raise``, ``load_config``
  1593→                will not catch any exceptions, leaving the charm to handle
  1594→                errors.
  1595→            args: positional arguments to pass through to the config class.
  1596→            kwargs: keyword arguments to pass through to the config class.
  1597→
  1598→        Returns:
  1599→            An instance of the config class that was passed in the ``cls`` argument
  1600→            with the current config values.
  1601→
  1602→        Raises:
  1603→            ValueError: if the configuration is invalid and ``errors`` is set to
  1604→                ``raise``.
  1605→        """
  1606→        from ._main import _Abort
  1607→
  1608→        config: dict[str, bool | int | float | str | model.Secret] = kwargs.copy()
  1609→        try:
  1610→            fields = set(_juju_fields(cls))
  1611→        except ValueError:
  1612→            fields = None
  1613→        for key, value in self.config.items():
  1614→            attr = key.replace('-', '_')
  1615→            if fields is not None and attr not in fields:
  1616→                continue
  1617→            option_type = self.meta.config.get(key)
  1618→            # Convert secret IDs to secret objects. We create the object rather
  1619→            # that using model.get_secret so that it's entirely lazy, in the
  1620→            # same way that SecretEvent.secret is.
  1621→            if option_type and option_type.type == 'secret':
  1622→                assert isinstance(value, str)  # Juju will have made sure of this.
  1623→                value = model.Secret(
  1624→                    backend=self.model._backend,
  1625→                    id=value,
  1626→                )
  1627→            config[attr] = value
  1628→        try:
  1629→            return cls(*args, **config)
  1630→        except ValueError as e:
  1631→            if errors == 'raise':
  1632→                raise
  1633→            # We exit with a zero code because we don't want Juju to retry
  1634→            # (the config needs to be fixed by the Juju user), and we don't
  1635→            # want the status we just set to be overridden by an error
  1636→            # status.
  1637→            self.unit.status = model.BlockedStatus(f'Invalid config: {e}')
  1638→            raise _Abort(0) from e
  1639→
  1640→
  1641→def _evaluate_status(charm: CharmBase):  # pyright: ignore[reportUnusedFunction]
  1642→    """Trigger collect-status events and evaluate and set the highest-priority status.
  1643→
  1644→    See :class:`CollectStatusEvent` for details.
  1645→    """
  1646→    if charm.framework.model._backend.is_leader():
  1647→        charm.on.collect_app_status.emit()
  1648→        app = charm.app
  1649→        if app._collected_statuses:
  1650→            app.status = model.StatusBase._get_highest_priority(app._collected_statuses)
  1651→
  1652→    charm.on.collect_unit_status.emit()
  1653→    unit = charm.unit
  1654→    if unit._collected_statuses:
  1655→        unit.status = model.StatusBase._get_highest_priority(unit._collected_statuses)
  1656→
  1657→
  1658→def _juju_fields(cls: type[object]) -> dict[str, str]:
  1659→    """Iterates over all the field names to include when loading into a class.
  1660→
  1661→    Any Juju names that are not in the returned dictionary should not be passed
  1662→    to the class. Names that are in the dictionary are mapped to the argument
  1663→    name; in most cases this is the same string, but for aliases will differ.
  1664→
  1665→    Returns:
  1666→        A dictionary where the key is the Juju name and the value is the name of
  1667→        the attribute in the Python class.
  1668→
  1669→    Raises:
  1670→        ValueError: if unable to determine which fields to include
  1671→    """
  1672→    # Dataclasses:
  1673→    juju_to_arg: dict[str, str] = {}
  1674→    if dataclasses.is_dataclass(cls):
  1675→        for field in dataclasses.fields(cls):
  1676→            alias = field.metadata.get('alias', field.name)
  1677→            # If this a Pydantic dataclass, then it handles the alias.
  1678→            # Using pydantic.dataclasses.is_pydantic_dataclass() would be
  1679→            # best here, but we don't want to import pydantic in ops, so
  1680→            # we look more explicitly.
  1681→            if getattr(cls, '__is_pydantic_dataclass__', False):
  1682→                juju_to_arg[alias] = alias
  1683→            else:
  1684→                juju_to_arg[alias] = field.name
  1685→        return juju_to_arg
  1686→    # Pydantic models:
  1687→    class_fields: dict[str, str] = {}
  1688→    if hasattr(cls, 'model_fields'):
  1689→        for name, field in cls.model_fields.items():  # type: ignore
  1690→            # Pydantic takes care of the alias.
  1691→            class_fields[field.alias or name] = field.alias or name  # type: ignore
  1692→        return class_fields
  1693→    # It's not clear, so give up.
  1694→    raise ValueError('Unable to find class fields')
  1695→
  1696→
  1697→class CharmMeta:
  1698→    """Object containing the metadata for the charm.
  1699→
  1700→    This is read from ``metadata.yaml``, ``config.yaml``, and ``actions.yaml``.
  1701→    Generally charms will define this information, rather than reading it at
  1702→    runtime. This class is mostly for the framework to understand what the charm
  1703→    has defined.
  1704→
  1705→    Args:
  1706→        raw: a mapping containing the contents of metadata.yaml
  1707→        actions_raw: a mapping containing the contents of actions.yaml
  1708→        config_raw: a mapping containing the contents of config.yaml
  1709→    """
  1710→
  1711→    name: str
  1712→    """Name of this charm."""
  1713→
  1714→    summary: str
  1715→    """Short description of what this charm does."""
  1716→
  1717→    description: str
  1718→    """Long description for this charm."""
  1719→
  1720→    maintainers: list[str]
  1721→    """List of email addresses of charm maintainers."""
  1722→
  1723→    links: MetadataLinks
  1724→    """Links to more details about the charm."""
  1725→
  1726→    tags: list[str]
  1727→    """Charmhub tag metadata for categories associated with this charm."""
  1728→
  1729→    terms: list[str]
  1730→    """Charmhub terms that should be agreed to before this charm can be deployed."""
  1731→
  1732→    series: list[str]
  1733→    """List of supported OS series that this charm can support.
  1734→
  1735→    The first entry in the list is the default series that will be used by
  1736→    deploy if no other series is requested by the user.
  1737→    """
  1738→
  1739→    subordinate: bool
  1740→    """Whether this charm is intended to be used as a subordinate charm."""
  1741→
  1742→    min_juju_version: str | None
  1743→    """Indicates the minimum Juju version this charm requires."""
  1744→
  1745→    assumes: JujuAssumes
  1746→    """Juju features this charm requires."""
  1747→
  1748→    charm_user: Literal['root', 'sudoer', 'non-root']
  1749→    """Type of user used to run the charm hook code.
  1750→
  1751→    The value of ``root`` ensures the charm runs as root. The value of
  1752→    ``sudoer`` runs the charm as a user other than root with access to sudo to
  1753→    elevate its privileges. ``non-root`` ensures the charm does not run as root
  1754→    and also does not have ``sudo`` privileges.
  1755→
  1756→    .. jujuadded 3.6.0
  1757→    """
  1758→
  1759→    containers: dict[str, ContainerMeta]
  1760→    """Container metadata for each defined container."""
  1761→
  1762→    requires: dict[str, RelationMeta]
  1763→    """Relations this charm requires."""
  1764→
  1765→    provides: dict[str, RelationMeta]
  1766→    """Relations this charm provides."""
  1767→
  1768→    peers: dict[str, RelationMeta]
  1769→    """Peer relations."""
  1770→
  1771→    relations: dict[str, RelationMeta]
  1772→    """All :class:`RelationMeta` instances.
  1773→
  1774→    This is merged from ``requires``, ``provides``, and ``peers``. If needed,
  1775→    the role of the relation definition can be obtained from its
  1776→    :attr:`role <RelationMeta.role>` attribute.
  1777→    """
  1778→
  1779→    storages: dict[str, StorageMeta]
  1780→    """Storage metadata for each defined storage."""
  1781→
  1782→    resources: dict[str, ResourceMeta]
  1783→    """Resource metadata for each defined resource."""
  1784→
  1785→    payloads: dict[str, PayloadMeta]
  1786→    """Payload metadata for each defined payload."""
  1787→
  1788→    extra_bindings: dict[str, None]
  1789→    """Additional named bindings that a charm can use for network configuration."""
  1790→
  1791→    actions: dict[str, ActionMeta]
  1792→    """Actions the charm has defined."""
  1793→
  1794→    config: dict[str, ConfigMeta]
  1795→    """Config options the charm has defined."""
  1796→
  1797→    def __init__(
  1798→        self,
  1799→        raw: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
  1800→        actions_raw: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
  1801→        config_raw: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
  1802→    ):
  1803→        raw_: dict[str, Any] = raw or {}
  1804→        actions_raw_: dict[str, Any] = actions_raw or {}
  1805→        # config_raw might be {'options': None} - this is accepted by
  1806→        # charmcraft, even though {'options: {}} would be more correct.
  1807→        options_raw: dict[str, Any] = (config_raw or {}).get('options') or {}
  1808→
  1809→        # When running in production, this data is generally loaded from
  1810→        # metadata.yaml. However, when running tests, this data is
  1811→        # potentially loaded from charmcraft.yaml (which will be split out
  1812→        # into a metadata.yaml as part of packing). Most of the field names
  1813→        # are the same, but there are some differences that we handle here,
  1814→        # and in _load_links(), so that loading from either file works.
  1815→        self.name = raw_.get('name', '')
  1816→        self.summary = raw_.get('summary', '')
  1817→        self.description = raw_.get('description', '')
  1818→        # The metadata spec says that these should be display-name <email>
  1819→        # (roughly 'name-addr' from RFC 5322). However, many charms have only
  1820→        # an email, or have a URL, or something else, so we leave these as
  1821→        # a plain string.
  1822→        self.maintainers: list[str] = []
  1823→        # Note that metadata v2 only defines 'maintainers' not 'maintainer'.
  1824→        if 'maintainer' in raw_:
  1825→            self.maintainers.append(raw_['maintainer'])
  1826→        if 'maintainers' in raw_:
  1827→            self.maintainers.extend(raw_['maintainers'])
  1828→        if 'links' in raw_ and 'contact' in raw_['links']:
  1829→            self.maintainers.append(raw_['links']['contact'])
  1830→        self._load_links(raw_)
  1831→        # Note that metadata v2 does not define tags.
  1832→        self.tags = raw_.get('tags', [])
  1833→        self.terms = raw_.get('terms', [])
  1834→        # Note that metadata v2 does not define series.
  1835→        self.series = raw_.get('series', [])
  1836→        self.subordinate = raw_.get('subordinate', False)
  1837→        self.assumes = JujuAssumes.from_list(raw_.get('assumes', []))
  1838→        # Note that metadata v2 does not define min-juju-version ('assumes'
  1839→        # should be used instead).
  1840→        self.min_juju_version = raw_.get('min-juju-version')
  1841→        self.charm_user = raw_.get('charm-user', 'root')
  1842→        self.requires = {
  1843→            name: RelationMeta(RelationRole.requires, name, rel)
  1844→            for name, rel in raw_.get('requires', {}).items()
  1845→        }
  1846→        self.provides = {
  1847→            name: RelationMeta(RelationRole.provides, name, rel)
  1848→            for name, rel in raw_.get('provides', {}).items()
  1849→        }
  1850→        self.peers = {
  1851→            name: RelationMeta(RelationRole.peer, name, rel)
  1852→            for name, rel in raw_.get('peers', {}).items()
  1853→        }
  1854→        self.relations: dict[str, RelationMeta] = {}
  1855→        self.relations.update(self.requires)
  1856→        self.relations.update(self.provides)
  1857→        self.relations.update(self.peers)
  1858→        self.storages = {
  1859→            name: StorageMeta(name, storage) for name, storage in raw_.get('storage', {}).items()
  1860→        }
  1861→        self.resources = {
  1862→            name: ResourceMeta(name, res) for name, res in raw_.get('resources', {}).items()
  1863→        }
  1864→        self.payloads = {
  1865→            name: PayloadMeta(name, payload) for name, payload in raw_.get('payloads', {}).items()
  1866→        }
  1867→        self.extra_bindings = raw_.get('extra-bindings', {})
  1868→        self.actions = {name: ActionMeta(name, action) for name, action in actions_raw_.items()}
  1869→        # This is predominately for backwards compatibility with Harness. In a
  1870→        # real Juju environment this shouldn't be possible, because charmcraft
  1871→        # validates the config when packing.
  1872→        for name, config in options_raw.items():
  1873→            if 'type' not in config:
  1874→                raise RuntimeError(
  1875→                    f'Incorrectly formatted config in YAML, option {name} is '
  1876→                    f'expected to declare a `type`.'
  1877→                )
  1878→        self.config = {
  1879→            name: ConfigMeta(
  1880→                name,
  1881→                type=config['type'],
  1882→                default=config.get('default'),
  1883→                description=config.get('description'),
  1884→            )
  1885→            for name, config in options_raw.items()
  1886→        }
  1887→        self.containers = {
  1888→            name: ContainerMeta(name, container)
  1889→            for name, container in raw_.get('containers', {}).items()
  1890→        }
  1891→
  1892→    @staticmethod
  1893→    def from_charm_root(charm_root: pathlib.Path | str):
  1894→        """Initialise CharmMeta from the path to a charm repository root folder."""
  1895→        charm_root = pathlib.Path(charm_root)
  1896→        metadata_path = charm_root / 'metadata.yaml'
  1897→
  1898→        with metadata_path.open() as f:
  1899→            meta = yaml.safe_load(f.read())
  1900→
  1901→        actions = None
  1902→        actions_path = charm_root / 'actions.yaml'
  1903→        if actions_path.exists():
  1904→            with actions_path.open() as f:
  1905→                actions = yaml.safe_load(f.read())
  1906→
  1907→        options = None
  1908→        config_path = charm_root / 'config.yaml'
  1909→        if config_path.exists():
  1910→            with config_path.open() as f:
  1911→                options = yaml.safe_load(f.read())
  1912→
  1913→        return CharmMeta(meta, actions, options)
  1914→
  1915→    def _load_links(self, raw: dict[str, Any]):
  1916→        websites = raw.get('website', [])
  1917→        if not websites and 'links' in raw:
  1918→            websites = raw['links'].get('website', [])
  1919→        # In YAML, this can be a single string, or a list of strings.
  1920→        if isinstance(websites, str):
  1921→            websites = [websites]
  1922→        sources = raw.get('source', [])
  1923→        if not sources and 'links' in raw:
  1924→            sources = raw['links'].get('source', [])
  1925→        # In YAML, this can be a single string, or a list of strings.
  1926→        if isinstance(sources, str):
  1927→            sources = [sources]
  1928→        issues = raw.get('issues', [])
  1929→        if not issues and 'links' in raw:
  1930→            issues = raw['links'].get('issues', [])
  1931→        # In YAML, this can be a single string, or a list of strings.
  1932→        if isinstance(issues, str):
  1933→            issues = [issues]
  1934→        documentation = raw.get('docs')
  1935→        if documentation is None:
  1936→            documentation = raw.get('links', {}).get('documentation')
  1937→        self.links = MetadataLinks(
  1938→            websites=websites,
  1939→            sources=sources,
  1940→            issues=issues,
  1941→            documentation=documentation,
  1942→        )
  1943→
  1944→    @classmethod
  1945→    def from_yaml(
  1946→        cls,
  1947→        metadata: str | TextIO,
  1948→        actions: str | TextIO | None = None,
  1949→        config: str | TextIO | None = None,
  1950→    ) -> CharmMeta:
  1951→        """Instantiate a :class:`CharmMeta` from a YAML description of ``metadata.yaml``.
  1952→
  1953→        Args:
  1954→            metadata: A YAML description of charm metadata (name, relations, etc.)
  1955→                This can be a simple string, or a file-like object (passed to ``yaml.safe_load``).
  1956→            actions: YAML description of Actions for this charm (e.g., actions.yaml)
  1957→            config: YAML description of Config for this charm (e.g., config.yaml)
  1958→        """
  1959→        meta = yaml.safe_load(metadata)
  1960→        raw_actions = {}
  1961→        if actions is not None:
  1962→            raw_actions = cast('dict[str, Any] | None', yaml.safe_load(actions))
  1963→            if raw_actions is None:
  1964→                raw_actions = {}
  1965→        raw_config = {}
  1966→        if config is not None:
  1967→            raw_config = cast('dict[str, Any] | None', yaml.safe_load(config))
  1968→            if raw_config is None:
  1969→                raw_config = {}
  1970→        return cls(meta, raw_actions, raw_config)
  1971→
  1972→
  1973→class RelationRole(enum.Enum):
  1974→    """An annotation for a charm's role in a relation.
  1975→
  1976→    For each relation a charm's role may be
  1977→
  1978→    - A Peer
  1979→    - A service consumer in the relation ('requires')
  1980→    - A service provider in the relation ('provides')
  1981→    """
  1982→
  1983→    peer = 'peer'
  1984→    requires = 'requires'
  1985→    provides = 'provides'
  1986→

  1995→class RelationMeta:
  1996→    """Object containing metadata about a relation definition.
  1997→
  1998→    Should not be constructed directly by charm code, but gotten from one of
  1999→    :attr:`CharmMeta.peers`, :attr:`CharmMeta.requires`, :attr:`CharmMeta.provides`,

2000→    or :attr:`CharmMeta.relations`.
  2001→    """
  2002→
  2003→    role: RelationRole
  2004→    """Role this relation takes, one of 'peer', 'requires', or 'provides'."""
  2005→
  2006→    relation_name: str
  2007→    """Name of this relation."""
  2008→
  2009→    interface_name: str | None
  2010→    """Definition of the interface protocol."""
  2011→
  2012→    limit: int | None
  2013→    """Maximum number of connections to this relation endpoint."""
  2014→
  2015→    scope: str
  2016→    """Scope based on how this relation should be used.
  2017→
  2018→    Will be either ``"global"`` or ``"container"``.
  2019→    """
  2020→
  2021→    optional: bool
  2022→    """If true, the relation is considered optional.
  2023→
  2024→    This value is informational only and is not used by Juju itself (all
  2025→    relations are optional from Juju's perspective), but it may be set in
  2026→    ``metadata.yaml`` and used by the charm code if appropriate.
  2027→    """
  2028→
  2029→    VALID_SCOPES: ClassVar[list[str]] = ['global', 'container']
  2030→
  2031→    def __init__(self, role: RelationRole, relation_name: str, raw: _RelationMetaDict):
  2032→        assert isinstance(role, RelationRole), (
  2033→            f'role should be one of {list(RelationRole)}, not {role!r}'
  2034→        )
  2035→        self._default_scope = self.VALID_SCOPES[0]
  2036→        self.role = role
  2037→        self.relation_name = relation_name
  2038→        self.interface_name = raw['interface']
  2039→
  2040→        self.limit = limit = raw.get('limit', None)
  2041→        if limit is not None and not isinstance(limit, int):
  2042→            raise TypeError(f'limit should be an int, not {type(limit)}')
  2043→
  2044→        self.scope = raw.get('scope') or self._default_scope
  2045→        if self.scope not in self.VALID_SCOPES:
  2046→            raise TypeError(
  2047→                "scope should be one of {}; not '{}'".format(
  2048→                    ', '.join(f"'{s}'" for s in self.VALID_SCOPES), self.scope
  2049→                )
  2050→            )
  2051→
  2052→        self.optional = raw.get('optional', False)

  2055→class StorageMeta:

  2085→    def __init__(self, name: str, raw: _StorageMetaDict):
  2086→        self.storage_name = name

  2091→        self.minimum_size = raw.get('minimum-size')

  2093→        self.multiple_range = None
  2094→        if 'multiple' in raw:
  2095→            range = raw['multiple']['range']
  2096→            if range[-1] == '+':
  2097→                self.multiple_range = (int(range[:-1]), None)
  2098→            elif '-' not in range:
  2099→                self.multiple_range = (int(range), int(range))
  2100→            else:
  2101→                range = range.split('-')
  2102→                self.multiple_range = (int(range[0]), int(range[1]) if range[1] else None)
  2103→        self.properties = raw.get('properties', [])

  2162→class JujuAssumesCondition(enum.Enum):
  2163→    """Distinguishes between :class:`JujuAssumes` that must match all or any features."""
  2164→
  2165→    ALL = 'all-of'
  2166→    """All features are required to satisfy the requirement."""
  2167→
  2168→    ANY = 'any-of'
  2169→    """Any of the features satisfies the requirement."""
  2170→
  2171→
  2172→@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
  2173→class JujuAssumes:
  2174→    """Juju model features that are required by the charm.
  2175→
  2176→    See the `Charmcraft docs <https://documentation.ubuntu.com/charmcraft/stable/reference/files/charmcraft-yaml-file/#assumes>`_
  2177→    for a list of available features.
  2178→    """
  2179→
  2180→    features: list[str | JujuAssumes]
  2181→    condition: JujuAssumesCondition = JujuAssumesCondition.ALL
  2182→
  2183→    @classmethod
  2184→    def from_list(
  2185→        cls,
  2186→        raw: list[Any],
  2187→        condition: JujuAssumesCondition = JujuAssumesCondition.ALL,
  2188→    ) -> JujuAssumes:
  2189→        """Create new JujuAssumes object from list parsed from YAML."""
  2190→        features: list[str | JujuAssumes] = []
  2191→        for feature in raw:
  2192→            if isinstance(feature, str):
  2193→                features.append(feature)
  2194→            else:
  2195→                for nested_condition, nested_features in feature.items():
  2196→                    features.append(
  2197→                        JujuAssumes.from_list(
  2198→                            nested_features, JujuAssumesCondition(nested_condition)
  2199→                        )
  2200→                    )
  2201→        return cls(features=features, condition=condition)

  2216→        self.additional_properties = raw.get('additionalProperties', juju_version < '4.0.0')

  2265→class ContainerMeta:
  2266→    """Metadata about an individual container."""
  2267→
  2268→    name: str
  2269→    """Name of the container (key in the YAML)."""
  2270→
  2271→    resource: str | None
  2272→    """Reference for an entry in the ``resources`` field.
  2273→
  2274→    Specifies the oci-image resource used to create the container. Must not be
  2275→    present if a base/channel is specified.
  2276→    """
  2277→
  2278→    bases: list[ContainerBase] | None
  2279→    """List of bases for use in resolving a container image.
  2280→
  2281→    Sorted by descending order of preference, and must not be present if
  2282→    resource is specified.
  2283→    """
  2284→
  2285→    def __init__(self, name: str, raw: dict[str, Any]):
  2286→        self.name = name
  2287→        self._mounts: dict[str, ContainerStorageMeta] = {}
  2288→        self.bases = None
  2289→        self.resource = None
  2290→
  2291→        # This is not guaranteed to be populated/is not enforced yet
  2292→        if raw:
  2293→            self._populate_mounts(raw.get('mounts', []))
  2294→            self.resource = raw.get('resource')
  2295→            self.bases = [ContainerBase.from_dict(base) for base in raw.get('bases', ())]
  2296→
  2297→        if self.resource and self.bases:
  2298→            raise model.ModelError('A container may specify a resource or base, not both.')
  2299→
  2300→    @property
  2301→    def mounts(self) -> dict[str, ContainerStorageMeta]:
  2302→        """An accessor for the mounts in a container.
  2303→
  2304→        Dict keys match key name in :class:`StorageMeta`
  2305→
  2306→        Example::
  2307→
  2308→            storage:
  2309→              foo:
  2310→                type: filesystem
  2311→                location: /test
  2312→            containers:
  2313→              bar:
  2314→                mounts:
  2315→                  - storage: foo
  2316→                  - location: /test/mount
  2317→        """
  2318→        return self._mounts
  2319→
  2320→    def _populate_mounts(self, mounts: list[_MountDict]):
  2321→        """Populate a list of container mountpoints.
  2322→
  2323→        Since Charm Metadata v2 specifies the mounts as a List, do a little data manipulation
  2324→        to convert the values to "friendly" names which contain a list of mountpoints
  2325→        under each key.
  2326→        """
  2327→        for mount in mounts:
  2328→            storage = mount.get('storage', '')
  2329→            mount = mount.get('location', '')
  2330→
  2331→            if not mount:
  2332→                continue
  2333→
  2334→            if storage in self._mounts:
  2335→                self._mounts[storage].add_location(mount)
  2336→            else:
  2337→                self._mounts[storage] = ContainerStorageMeta(storage, mount)
  2338→

  2340→class ContainerStorageMeta:
  2341→    """Metadata about storage for an individual container.
  2342→
  2343→    If multiple locations are specified for the same storage, such as Kubernetes subPath mounts,
  2344→    ``location`` will not be an accessible attribute, as it would not be possible to determine
  2345→    which mount point was desired, and ``locations`` should be iterated over.
  2346→    """
  2347→
  2348→    storage: str
  2349→    """Name for the mount point, which should exist in the keys of the charm's
  2350→    :class:`StorageMeta`.
  2351→    """
  2352→
  2353→    def __init__(self, storage: str, location: str):
  2354→        self.storage = storage
  2355→        self._locations: list[str] = [location]
  2356→
  2357→    def add_location(self, location: str):
  2358→        """Add an additional mount point to a known storage."""
  2359→        self._locations.append(location)
  2360→
  2361→    @property
  2362→    def locations(self) -> list[str]:
  2363→        """An accessor for the list of locations for a mount."""
  2364→        return self._locations
  2365→
  2366→    @property
  2367→    def location(self) -> str:
  2368→        """The location the storage is mounted at.
  2369→
  2370→        Raises:
  2371→            RuntimeError: if there is more than one mount point with the same
  2372→                backing storage - use :attr:`locations` instead.
  2373→        """
  2374→        if len(self._locations) == 1:
  2375→            return self._locations[0]
  2376→        raise RuntimeError(
  2377→            'container has more than one mount point with the same backing storage. '
  2378→            'Request .locations to see a list'
  2379→        )

   357→class _NotProvidedFlag:
   358→    pass
   359→
   360→
   361→_not_provided = _NotProvidedFlag()
   362→
   363→
   364→class _UnixSocketConnection(http.client.HTTPConnection):
   365→    """Implementation of HTTPConnection that connects to a named Unix socket."""
   366→
   367→    def __init__(
   368→        self, host: str, socket_path: str, timeout: _NotProvidedFlag | float = _not_provided
   369→    ):
   370→        if timeout is _not_provided:
   371→            super().__init__(host)
   372→        else:
   373→            assert isinstance(timeout, (int, float)), timeout  # type guard for pyright
   374→            super().__init__(host, timeout=timeout)
   375→        self.socket_path = socket_path
   376→
   377→    def connect(self):
   378→        """Override connect to use Unix socket (instead of TCP socket)."""
   379→        if not hasattr(socket, 'AF_UNIX'):
   380→            raise NotImplementedError(f'Unix sockets not supported on {sys.platform}')
   381→        self.sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_UNIX, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
   382→        self.sock.connect(self.socket_path)
   383→        if self.timeout is not _not_provided:
   384→            self.sock.settimeout(self.timeout)
   385→
   386→
   387→class _UnixSocketHandler(urllib.request.AbstractHTTPHandler):
   388→    """Implementation of HTTPHandler that uses a named Unix socket."""
   389→
   390→    def __init__(self, socket_path: str):
   391→        super().__init__()
   392→        self.socket_path = socket_path
   393→
   394→    def http_open(self, req: urllib.request.Request):
   395→        """Override http_open to use a Unix socket connection (instead of TCP)."""
   396→        return self.do_open(
   397→            _UnixSocketConnection,  # type:ignore
   398→            req,
   399→            socket_path=self.socket_path,
   400→        )

   405→
   406→    The format is in seconds with a millisecond resolution and an 's' suffix,
   407→    as accepted by the Pebble API (which uses Go's time.ParseDuration).
   408→    """
   409→    return f'{timeout:.3f}s'

   467→
   468→    status: str

   470→

   473→
   474→    def __init__(self, body: dict[str, Any], code: int, status: str, message: str):
   475→        """This shouldn't be instantiated directly."""
   476→        super().__init__(message)  # Makes str(e) return message
   477→        self.body = body
   478→        self.code = code
   479→        self.status = status
   480→        self.message = message
