   486→class ChangeError(Error):
   487→    """Raised by actions when a change is ready but has an error."""
   488→
   489→    err: str
   490→    """Human-readable error message."""
   491→
   492→    change: Change
   493→    """Change object associated with this error."""
   494→
   495→    def __init__(self, err: str, change: Change):
   496→        """This shouldn't be instantiated directly."""
   497→        self.err = err
   498→        self.change = change
   499→
   500→    def __str__(self):
   501→        parts = [self.err]
   502→
   503→        # Append any task logs to the error message
   504→        for i, task in enumerate(self.change.tasks):
   505→            if not task.log:
   506→                continue
   507→            parts.append(f'\n----- Logs from task {i} -----\n')
   508→            parts.append('\n'.join(task.log))
   509→
   510→        if len(parts) > 1:
   511→            parts.append('\n-----')
   512→
   513→        return ''.join(parts)
   514→
   515→    def __repr__(self):
   516→        return f'ChangeError({self.err!r}, {self.change!r})'
   517→

   519→class ExecError(Error, Generic[AnyStr]):
   520→    """Raised when a :meth:`Client.exec` command returns a non-zero exit code."""
   521→
   522→    STR_MAX_OUTPUT = 1024
   523→    """Maximum number of characters that stdout/stderr are truncated to in ``__str__``."""
   524→
   525→    command: list[str]
   526→    """Command line of command being executed."""
   527→
   528→    exit_code: int
   529→    """The process's exit code. Because this is an error, this will always be non-zero."""
   530→
   531→    stdout: AnyStr | None
   532→    """Standard output from the process.
   533→
   534→    If :meth:`ExecProcess.wait_output` was being called, this is the captured
   535→    stdout as a str (or bytes if encoding was None). If :meth:`ExecProcess.wait`
   536→    was being called, this is None.
   537→    """
   538→
   539→    stderr: AnyStr | None
   540→    """Standard error from the process.
   541→
   542→    If :meth:`ExecProcess.wait_output` was being called and ``combine_stderr``
   543→    was ``False``, this is the captured stderr as a str (or bytes if encoding was
   544→    None). If :meth:`ExecProcess.wait` was being called or ``combine_stderr``
   545→    was ``True``, this is None.
   546→    """
   547→
   548→    def __init__(
   549→        self,
   550→        command: list[str],
   551→        exit_code: int,
   552→        stdout: AnyStr | None,
   553→        stderr: AnyStr | None,
   554→    ):
   555→        self.command = command
   556→        self.exit_code = exit_code
   557→        self.stdout = stdout
   558→        self.stderr = stderr
   559→
   560→    def __str__(self):
   561→        message = f'non-zero exit code {self.exit_code} executing {self.command[0]!r}'
   562→
   563→        for name, out in [('stdout', self.stdout), ('stderr', self.stderr)]:
   564→            if out is None:
   565→                continue
   566→            truncated = ' [truncated]' if len(out) > self.STR_MAX_OUTPUT else ''
   567→            out = out[: self.STR_MAX_OUTPUT]
   568→            message = f'{message}, {name}={out!r}{truncated}'
   569→
   570→        return message
   571→

   812→class Plan:
   813→    """Represents the effective Pebble configuration.
   814→
   815→    A plan is the combined layer configuration. The layer configuration is
   816→    documented at
   817→    https://documentation.ubuntu.com/pebble/reference/layer-specification/
   818→    """
   819→
   820→    def __init__(self, raw: str | PlanDict | None = None):
   821→        if isinstance(raw, str):  # noqa: SIM108
   822→            d = yaml.safe_load(raw) or {}  # type: ignore
   823→        else:
   824→            d = raw or {}
   825→        d = typing.cast('PlanDict', d)
   826→
   827→        self._raw = raw
   828→        self._services: dict[str, Service] = {
   829→            name: Service(name, service) for name, service in d.get('services', {}).items()
   830→        }
   831→        self._checks: dict[str, Check] = {
   832→            name: Check(name, check) for name, check in d.get('checks', {}).items()
   833→        }
   834→        self._log_targets: dict[str, LogTarget] = {
   835→            name: LogTarget(name, target) for name, target in d.get('log-targets', {}).items()
   836→        }
   837→
   838→    @property
   839→    def services(self) -> dict[str, Service]:
   840→        """This plan's services mapping (maps service name to Service).
   841→
   842→        This property is currently read-only.
   843→        """
   844→        return self._services
   845→
   846→    @property
   847→    def checks(self) -> dict[str, Check]:
   848→        """This plan's checks mapping (maps check name to :class:`Check`).
   849→
   850→        This property is currently read-only.
   851→        """
   852→        return self._checks
   853→
   854→    @property
   855→    def log_targets(self) -> dict[str, LogTarget]:
   856→        """This plan's log targets mapping (maps log target name to :class:`LogTarget`).
   857→
   858→        This property is currently read-only.
   859→        """
   860→        return self._log_targets
   861→
   862→    def to_dict(self) -> PlanDict:
   863→        """Convert this plan to its dict representation."""
   864→        fields = [
   865→            ('services', {name: service.to_dict() for name, service in self._services.items()}),
   866→            ('checks', {name: check.to_dict() for name, check in self._checks.items()}),
   867→            (
   868→                'log-targets',
   869→                {name: target.to_dict() for name, target in self._log_targets.items()},
   870→            ),
   871→        ]
   872→        dct = {name: value for name, value in fields if value}
   873→        return typing.cast('PlanDict', dct)
   874→
   875→    def to_yaml(self) -> str:
   876→        """Return this plan's YAML representation."""
   877→        return yaml.safe_dump(self.to_dict())
   878→
   879→    __str__ = to_yaml
   880→
   881→    def __repr__(self):
   882→        return f'Plan({self.to_dict()!r})'
   883→
   884→    def __eq__(self, other: PlanDict | Plan) -> bool:
   885→        if isinstance(other, dict):
   886→            return self.to_dict() == other
   887→        elif isinstance(other, Plan):
   888→            return self.to_dict() == other.to_dict()
   889→        return NotImplemented
   890→

   892→class Layer:
   893→    """Represents a Pebble configuration layer.
   894→
   895→    The format of this is documented at
   896→    https://documentation.ubuntu.com/pebble/reference/layer-specification/
   897→    """
   898→
   899→    #: Summary of the purpose of this layer.
   900→    summary: str
   901→    #: Long-form description of this layer.
   902→    description: str
   903→    #: Mapping of name to :class:`Service` defined by this layer.
   904→    services: dict[str, Service]
   905→    #: Mapping of check to :class:`Check` defined by this layer.
   906→    checks: dict[str, Check]
   907→    #: Mapping of target to :class:`LogTarget` defined by this layer.
   908→    log_targets: dict[str, LogTarget]
   909→
   910→    def __init__(self, raw: str | LayerDict | None = None):
   911→        if isinstance(raw, str):  # noqa: SIM108
   912→            d = yaml.safe_load(raw) or {}  # type: ignore # (Any 'raw' type)
   913→        else:
   914→            d = raw or {}
   915→        d = typing.cast('LayerDict', d)
   916→
   917→        self.summary = d.get('summary', '')
   918→        self.description = d.get('description', '')
   919→        self.services = {
   920→            name: Service(name, service) for name, service in d.get('services', {}).items()
   921→        }
   922→        self.checks = {name: Check(name, check) for name, check in d.get('checks', {}).items()}
   923→        self.log_targets = {
   924→            name: LogTarget(name, target) for name, target in d.get('log-targets', {}).items()
   925→        }
   926→
   927→    def to_yaml(self) -> str:
   928→        """Convert this layer to its YAML representation."""
   929→        return yaml.safe_dump(self.to_dict())
   930→
   931→    def to_dict(self) -> LayerDict:
   932→        """Convert this layer to its dict representation."""
   933→        fields = [
   934→            ('summary', self.summary),
   935→            ('description', self.description),
   936→            ('services', {name: service.to_dict() for name, service in self.services.items()}),
   937→            ('checks', {name: check.to_dict() for name, check in self.checks.items()}),
   938→            ('log-targets', {name: target.to_dict() for name, target in self.log_targets.items()}),
   939→        ]
   940→        dct = {name: value for name, value in fields if value}
   941→        return typing.cast('LayerDict', dct)
   942→
   943→    def __repr__(self) -> str:
   944→        return f'Layer({self.to_dict()!r})'
   945→
   946→    def __eq__(self, other: LayerDict | Layer) -> bool:
   947→        """Reports whether this layer configuration is equal to another."""
   948→        if isinstance(other, dict):
   949→            return self.to_dict() == other
   950→        elif isinstance(other, Layer):
   951→            return self.to_dict() == other.to_dict()
   952→        else:
   953→            return NotImplemented
   954→

   996→            ('requires', self.requires),
   997→            ('environment', self.environment),
   998→            ('user', self.user),
   999→            ('user-id', self.user_id),

1000→            ('group', self.group),
  1001→            ('group-id', self.group_id),
  1002→            ('working-dir', self.working_dir),
  1003→            ('on-success', self.on_success),
  1004→            ('on-failure', self.on_failure),
  1005→            ('on-check-failure', self.on_check_failure),
  1006→            ('backoff-delay', self.backoff_delay),

  1014→    def _merge(self, other: Service):
  1015→        """Merges this service object with another service definition.
  1016→
  1017→        For attributes present in both objects, the passed in service
  1018→        attributes take precedence.
  1019→        """
  1020→        for name, value in other.__dict__.items():
  1021→            if not value or name == 'name':
  1022→                continue

  1025→            elif name in ['environment', 'on_check_failure']:

  1027→            else:

  1037→        elif isinstance(other, Service):

  1096→class Check:
  1097→    """Represents a check in a Pebble configuration layer."""
  1098→
  1099→    def __init__(self, name: str, raw: CheckDict | None = None):
  1100→        self.name = name
  1101→        dct: CheckDict = raw or {}
  1102→        self.override: str = dct.get('override', '')
  1103→        try:
  1104→            level: CheckLevel | str = CheckLevel(dct.get('level', ''))
  1105→        except ValueError:
  1106→            level = dct.get('level', '')
  1107→        self.level = level
  1108→        self.startup = CheckStartup(dct.get('startup', ''))
  1109→        self.period: str | None = dct.get('period', '')
  1110→        self.timeout: str | None = dct.get('timeout', '')
  1111→        self.threshold: int | None = dct.get('threshold')
  1112→
  1113→        http = dct.get('http')
  1114→        if http is not None:
  1115→            http = copy.deepcopy(http)
  1116→        self.http: HttpDict | None = http
  1117→
  1118→        tcp = dct.get('tcp')
  1119→        if tcp is not None:
  1120→            tcp = copy.deepcopy(tcp)
  1121→        self.tcp: TcpDict | None = tcp
  1122→
  1123→        exec_ = dct.get('exec')
  1124→        if exec_ is not None:
  1125→            exec_ = copy.deepcopy(exec_)
  1126→        self.exec: ExecDict | None = exec_
  1127→
  1128→    def to_dict(self) -> CheckDict:
  1129→        """Convert this check object to its dict representation."""
  1130→        level: str = self.level.value if isinstance(self.level, CheckLevel) else self.level
  1131→        fields = [
  1132→            ('override', self.override),
  1133→            ('level', level),
  1134→            ('startup', self.startup.value),
  1135→            ('period', self.period),
  1136→            ('timeout', self.timeout),
  1137→            ('threshold', self.threshold),
  1138→            ('http', self.http),
  1139→            ('tcp', self.tcp),
  1140→            ('exec', self.exec),
  1141→        ]
  1142→        dct = {name: value for name, value in fields if value}
  1143→        return typing.cast('CheckDict', dct)
  1144→
  1145→    def __repr__(self) -> str:
  1146→        return f'Check({self.to_dict()!r})'
  1147→
  1148→    def __eq__(self, other: CheckDict | Check) -> bool:
  1149→        """Reports whether this check configuration is equal to another."""
  1150→        if isinstance(other, dict):
  1151→            return self.to_dict() == other
  1152→        elif isinstance(other, Check):
  1153→            return self.to_dict() == other.to_dict()
  1154→        else:
  1155→            return NotImplemented
  1156→
  1157→    def _merge_exec(self, other: ExecDict) -> None:
  1158→        """Merges this exec object with another exec definition.
  1159→
  1160→        For attributes present in both objects, the passed in exec
  1161→        attributes take precedence.
  1162→        """
  1163→        if self.exec is None:
  1164→            self.exec = {}
  1165→        for name, value in other.items():
  1166→            if not value:
  1167→                continue
  1168→            if name == 'environment':
  1169→                current = self.exec.get(name, {})
  1170→                current.update(value)  # type: ignore
  1171→                self.exec[name] = current
  1172→            else:
  1173→                self.exec[name] = value
  1174→
  1175→    def _merge_http(self, other: HttpDict) -> None:
  1176→        """Merges this http object with another http definition.
  1177→
  1178→        For attributes present in both objects, the passed in http
  1179→        attributes take precedence.
  1180→        """
  1181→        if self.http is None:
  1182→            self.http = {}
  1183→        for name, value in other.items():
  1184→            if not value:
  1185→                continue
  1186→            if name == 'headers':
  1187→                current = self.http.get(name, {})
  1188→                current.update(value)  # type: ignore
  1189→                self.http[name] = current
  1190→            else:
  1191→                self.http[name] = value
  1192→
  1193→    def _merge_tcp(self, other: TcpDict) -> None:
  1194→        """Merges this tcp object with another tcp definition.
  1195→
  1196→        For attributes present in both objects, the passed in tcp
  1197→        attributes take precedence.
  1198→        """
  1199→        if self.tcp is None:
  1200→            self.tcp = {}
  1201→        for name, value in other.items():
  1202→            if not value:
  1203→                continue
  1204→            self.tcp[name] = value
  1205→
  1206→    def _merge(self, other: Check):
  1207→        """Merges this check object with another check definition.
  1208→
  1209→        For attributes present in both objects, the passed in check
  1210→        attributes take precedence.
  1211→        """
  1212→        for name, value in other.__dict__.items():
  1213→            # 'not value' is safe here because a threshold of 0 is valid but
  1214→            # inconsistently applied and not of any actual use, and the other
  1215→            # fields are strings where the empty string means 'not in the layer'.
  1216→            if not value or name == 'name':
  1217→                continue
  1218→            if name == 'http':
  1219→                self._merge_http(value)
  1220→            elif name == 'tcp':
  1221→                self._merge_tcp(value)
  1222→            elif name == 'exec':
  1223→                self._merge_exec(value)
  1224→            elif name == 'startup' and value == CheckStartup.UNSET:
  1225→                continue
  1226→            # Note that CheckLevel.UNSET has a different meaning to
  1227→            # CheckStartup.UNSET. In the former, it means 'there is no level';
  1228→            # in the latter it means 'use the default'.
  1229→            else:
  1230→                setattr(self, name, value)
  1231→

  1416→class CheckInfo:
  1417→    """Check status information.
  1418→
  1419→    A list of these objects is returned from :meth:`Client.get_checks`.
  1420→    """
  1421→
  1422→    name: str
  1423→    """Name of the check."""
  1424→
  1425→    level: CheckLevel | str | None
  1426→    """Check level.
  1427→
  1428→    This can be :attr:`CheckLevel.ALIVE`, :attr:`CheckLevel.READY`, or ``None`` (level not set).
  1429→    """
  1430→
  1431→    startup: CheckStartup
  1432→    """Startup mode.
  1433→
  1434→    :attr:`CheckStartup.ENABLED` means the check will be started when added, and
  1435→    in a replan. :attr:`CheckStartup.DISABLED` means the check must be manually
  1436→    started.
  1437→    """
  1438→
  1439→    status: CheckStatus | str
  1440→    """Status of the check.
  1441→
  1442→    :attr:`CheckStatus.UP` means the check is healthy (the number of failures
  1443→    is less than the threshold), :attr:`CheckStatus.DOWN` means the check is
  1444→    unhealthy (the number of failures has reached the threshold), and
  1445→    :attr:`CheckStatus.INACTIVE` means the check is not running.
  1446→    """
  1447→
  1448→    successes: int | None
  1449→    """Number of times this check has succeeded.
  1450→
  1451→    This will be zero if the check has never run successfully after being started,
  1452→    either on Pebble startup through `startup: enabled`, or due to
  1453→    :meth:`Client.start_checks`. It is reset to one when the check
  1454→    succeeds after the check's failure threshold was reached.
  1455→
  1456→    This will be ``None`` if the version of Pebble being queried doesn't return
  1457→    the ``successes`` field (introduced in Pebble v1.23.0).
  1458→    """
  1459→
  1460→    failures: int
  1461→    """Number of failures since the check last succeeded.
  1462→
  1463→    This is reset to zero if the check succeeds.
  1464→    """
  1465→
  1466→    threshold: int
  1467→    """Failure threshold.
  1468→
  1469→    This is how many consecutive failures for the check to be considered "down".
  1470→    """
  1471→
  1472→    change_id: ChangeID | None
  1473→    """Change ID of ``perform-check`` or ``recover-check`` change driving this check.
  1474→
  1475→    This will be ``None`` on older versions of Pebble, which did not use changes
  1476→    to drive health checks.
  1477→    """
  1478→
  1479→    def __init__(
  1480→        self,
  1481→        name: str,
  1482→        level: CheckLevel | str | None,
  1483→        status: CheckStatus | str,
  1484→        successes: int | None = None,
  1485→        failures: int = 0,
  1486→        threshold: int = 0,
  1487→        change_id: ChangeID | None = None,
  1488→        startup: CheckStartup = CheckStartup.ENABLED,
  1489→    ):
  1490→        self.name = name
  1491→        self.level = level
  1492→        self.startup = startup
  1493→        self.status = status
  1494→        self.successes = successes
  1495→        self.failures = failures
  1496→        self.threshold = threshold
  1497→        self.change_id = change_id
  1498→
  1499→    @classmethod
  1500→    def from_dict(cls, d: _CheckInfoDict) -> CheckInfo:
  1501→        """Create new :class:`CheckInfo` object from dict parsed from JSON."""
  1502→        try:
  1503→            level = CheckLevel(d.get('level', ''))
  1504→        except ValueError:
  1505→            level = d.get('level')
  1506→        try:
  1507→            status = CheckStatus(d['status'])
  1508→        except ValueError:
  1509→            status = d['status']
  1510→        change_id = ChangeID(d['change-id']) if 'change-id' in d else None
  1511→        if not change_id and 'startup' in d:
  1512→            # This is a version of Pebble that supports stopping checks, which
  1513→            # means that the check is inactive if it has no change ID.
  1514→            status = CheckStatus.INACTIVE
  1515→        return cls(
  1516→            name=d['name'],
  1517→            level=level,
  1518→            startup=CheckStartup(d.get('startup', 'enabled')),
  1519→            status=status,
  1520→            successes=d.get('successes'),
  1521→            failures=d.get('failures', 0),
  1522→            threshold=d['threshold'],
  1523→            change_id=change_id,
  1524→        )
  1525→
  1526→    def __repr__(self):
  1527→        return (
  1528→            'CheckInfo('
  1529→            f'name={self.name!r}, '
  1530→            f'level={self.level}, '
  1531→            f'startup={self.startup}, '
  1532→            f'status={self.status}, '
  1533→            f'successes={self.successes}, '
  1534→            f'failures={self.failures}, '
  1535→            f'threshold={self.threshold!r}, '
  1536→            f'change_id={self.change_id!r})'
  1537→        )
  1538→
  1539→    @property
  1540→    def has_run(self):
  1541→        """Report whether this check has run at least once (successfully or otherwise).
  1542→
  1543→        Raises:
  1544→            NotImplementedError: If running against a version of Pebble too
  1545→                old to support the "successes" field.
  1546→        """
  1547→        if self.successes is None:
  1548→            raise NotImplementedError(
  1549→                'Pebble version doesn\'t support "successes" (introduced in Pebble 1.23.0)'
  1550→            )
  1551→        return self.successes > 0 or self.failures > 0
  1552→

  1591→class ChangeStatus(enum.Enum):

  1624→@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
  1625→class Notice:
  1626→    """Information about a single notice."""
  1627→
  1628→    id: str
  1629→    """Server-generated unique ID for this notice."""
  1630→
  1631→    user_id: int | None
  1632→    """UID of the user who may view this notice (None means notice is public)."""
  1633→
  1634→    type: NoticeType | str
  1635→    """Type of the notice."""
  1636→
  1637→    key: str
  1638→    """The notice key, a string that differentiates notices of this type.
  1639→
  1640→    This is in the format ``example.com/path``.
  1641→    """
  1642→
  1643→    first_occurred: datetime.datetime
  1644→    """The first time one of these notices (type and key combination) occurs."""
  1645→
  1646→    last_occurred: datetime.datetime
  1647→    """The last time one of these notices occurred."""
  1648→
  1649→    last_repeated: datetime.datetime
  1650→    """The time this notice was last repeated.
  1651→
  1652→    See Pebble's `Notices documentation <https://documentation.ubuntu.com/pebble/reference/notices/>`_
  1653→    for an explanation of what "repeated" means.
  1654→    """
  1655→
  1656→    occurrences: int
  1657→    """The number of times one of these notices has occurred."""
  1658→
  1659→    last_data: dict[str, str] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=dict[str, str])
  1660→    """Additional data captured from the last occurrence of one of these notices."""
  1661→
  1662→    repeat_after: datetime.timedelta | None = None
  1663→    """Minimum time after one of these was last repeated before Pebble will repeat it again."""
  1664→
  1665→    expire_after: datetime.timedelta | None = None
  1666→    """How long since one of these last occurred until Pebble will drop the notice."""
  1667→
  1668→    @classmethod
  1669→    def from_dict(cls, d: _NoticeDict) -> Notice:
  1670→        """Create new Notice object from dict parsed from JSON."""
  1671→        try:
  1672→            notice_type = NoticeType(d['type'])
  1673→        except ValueError:
  1674→            notice_type = d['type']
  1675→        return cls(
  1676→            id=d['id'],
  1677→            user_id=d.get('user-id'),
  1678→            type=notice_type,
  1679→            key=d['key'],
  1680→            first_occurred=timeconv.parse_rfc3339(d['first-occurred']),
  1681→            last_occurred=timeconv.parse_rfc3339(d['last-occurred']),
  1682→            last_repeated=timeconv.parse_rfc3339(d['last-repeated']),
  1683→            occurrences=d['occurrences'],
  1684→            last_data=d.get('last-data') or {},
  1685→            repeat_after=timeconv.parse_duration(d['repeat-after'])
  1686→            if 'repeat-after' in d
  1687→            else None,
  1688→            expire_after=timeconv.parse_duration(d['expire-after'])
  1689→            if 'expire-after' in d
  1690→            else None,
  1691→        )

  1694→class ExecProcess(Generic[AnyStr]):
  1695→    """Represents a process started by :meth:`Client.exec`.
  1696→
  1697→    To avoid deadlocks, most users should use :meth:`wait_output` instead of
  1698→    reading and writing the :attr:`stdin`, :attr:`stdout`, and :attr:`stderr`
  1699→    attributes directly. Alternatively, users can pass stdin/stdout/stderr to
  1700→    :meth:`Client.exec`.
  1701→
  1702→    This class should not be instantiated directly, only via
  1703→    :meth:`Client.exec`.
  1704→    """
  1705→
  1706→    stdin: IO[AnyStr] | None
  1707→    """Standard input for the process.
  1708→
  1709→    If the stdin argument was not passed to :meth:`Client.exec`, this is a
  1710→    writable file-like object the caller can use to stream input to the
  1711→    process. It is ``None`` if stdin was passed to :meth:`Client.exec`.
  1712→    """
  1713→
  1714→    stdout: IO[AnyStr] | None
  1715→    """Standard output from the process.
  1716→
  1717→    If the stdout argument was not passed to :meth:`Client.exec`, this is a
  1718→    readable file-like object the caller can use to stream output from the
  1719→    process. It is ``None`` if stdout was passed to :meth:`Client.exec`.
  1720→    """
  1721→
  1722→    stderr: IO[AnyStr] | None
  1723→    """Standard error from the process.
  1724→
  1725→    If the stderr argument was not passed to :meth:`Client.exec` and
  1726→    ``combine_stderr`` was ``False``, this is a readable file-like object the
  1727→    caller can use to stream error output from the process. It is ``None`` if
  1728→    stderr was passed to :meth:`Client.exec` or ``combine_stderr`` was ``True``.
  1729→    """
  1730→
  1731→    def __init__(
  1732→        self,
  1733→        stdin: IO[AnyStr] | None,
  1734→        stdout: IO[AnyStr] | None,
  1735→        stderr: IO[AnyStr] | None,
  1736→        client: Client,
  1737→        timeout: float | None,
  1738→        control_ws: _WebSocket,
  1739→        stdio_ws: _WebSocket,
  1740→        stderr_ws: _WebSocket | None,
  1741→        command: list[str],
  1742→        encoding: str | None,
  1743→        change_id: ChangeID,
  1744→        cancel_stdin: Callable[[], None] | None,
  1745→        cancel_reader: int | None,
  1746→        threads: list[threading.Thread],
  1747→    ):
  1748→        self.stdin = stdin
  1749→        self.stdout = stdout
  1750→        self.stderr = stderr
  1751→        self._client = client
  1752→        self._timeout = timeout
  1753→        self._control_ws = control_ws
  1754→        self._stdio_ws = stdio_ws
  1755→        self._stderr_ws = stderr_ws
  1756→        self._command = command
  1757→        self._encoding = encoding
  1758→        self._change_id = change_id
  1759→        self._cancel_stdin = cancel_stdin
  1760→        self._cancel_reader = cancel_reader
  1761→        self._threads = threads
  1762→        self._waited = False
  1763→
  1764→    def __del__(self):
  1765→        if not self._waited:
  1766→            msg = 'ExecProcess instance garbage collected without call to wait() or wait_output()'
  1767→            warnings.warn(msg, ResourceWarning)
  1768→
  1769→    def wait(self):
  1770→        """Wait for the process to finish.
  1771→
  1772→        If a timeout was specified to the :meth:`Client.exec` call, this waits
  1773→        at most that duration.
  1774→
  1775→        Raises:
  1776→            ChangeError: if there was an error starting or running the process.
  1777→            ExecError: if the process exits with a non-zero exit code.
  1778→        """
  1779→        with tracer.start_as_current_span('pebble wait'):
  1780→            exit_code = self._wait()
  1781→            if exit_code != 0:
  1782→                raise ExecError(self._command, exit_code, None, None)
  1783→
  1784→    def _wait(self) -> int:
  1785→        self._waited = True
  1786→        timeout = self._timeout
  1787→        if timeout is not None:
  1788→            # A bit more than the command timeout to ensure that happens first
  1789→            timeout += 1
  1790→        change = self._client.wait_change(self._change_id, timeout=timeout)
  1791→
  1792→        # If stdin reader thread is running, stop it
  1793→        if self._cancel_stdin is not None:
  1794→            self._cancel_stdin()
  1795→
  1796→        # Wait for all threads to finish (e.g., message barrier sent)
  1797→        for thread in self._threads:
  1798→            thread.join()
  1799→

1800→        # If we opened a cancel_reader pipe, close the read side now (write
  1801→        # side was already closed by _cancel_stdin().
  1802→        if self._cancel_reader is not None:
  1803→            os.close(self._cancel_reader)
  1804→
  1805→        # Close websockets (shutdown doesn't send CLOSE message or wait for response).
  1806→        self._control_ws.shutdown()
  1807→        self._stdio_ws.shutdown()
  1808→        if self._stderr_ws is not None:
  1809→            self._stderr_ws.shutdown()
  1810→
  1811→        if change.err:
  1812→            raise ChangeError(change.err, change)
  1813→
  1814→        exit_code = -1
  1815→        if change.tasks:
  1816→            exit_code = change.tasks[0].data.get('exit-code', -1)
  1817→        return exit_code
  1818→
  1819→    def wait_output(self) -> tuple[AnyStr, AnyStr | None]:
  1820→        """Wait for the process to finish and return tuple of (stdout, stderr).
  1821→
  1822→        If a timeout was specified to the :meth:`Client.exec` call, this waits
  1823→        at most that duration. If combine_stderr was ``True``, stdout will include
  1824→        the process's standard error, and stderr will be ``None``.
  1825→
  1826→        Raises:
  1827→            ChangeError: if there was an error starting or running the process.
  1828→            ExecError: if the process exits with a non-zero exit code.
  1829→            TypeError: if :meth:`Client.exec` was called with the ``stdout`` argument.
  1830→        """
  1831→        with tracer.start_as_current_span('pebble wait_output'):
  1832→            if self.stdout is None:
  1833→                raise TypeError(
  1834→                    "can't use wait_output() when exec was called with the stdout argument; "
  1835→                    'use wait() instead'
  1836→                )
  1837→
  1838→            if self._encoding is not None:
  1839→                out = io.StringIO()
  1840→                err = io.StringIO() if self.stderr is not None else None
  1841→            else:
  1842→                out = io.BytesIO()
  1843→                err = io.BytesIO() if self.stderr is not None else None
  1844→
  1845→            t = _start_thread(shutil.copyfileobj, self.stdout, out)
  1846→            self._threads.append(t)
  1847→
  1848→            if self.stderr is not None:
  1849→                t = _start_thread(shutil.copyfileobj, self.stderr, err)
  1850→                self._threads.append(t)
  1851→
  1852→            exit_code: int = self._wait()
  1853→
  1854→            out_value = typing.cast('AnyStr', out.getvalue())
  1855→            err_value = typing.cast('AnyStr', err.getvalue()) if err is not None else None
  1856→            if exit_code != 0:
  1857→                raise ExecError[AnyStr](self._command, exit_code, out_value, err_value)
  1858→
  1859→            return (out_value, err_value)
  1860→
  1861→    def send_signal(self, sig: int | str):
  1862→        """Send the given signal to the running process.
  1863→
  1864→        Args:
  1865→            sig: Name or number of signal to send, e.g., "SIGHUP", 1, or
  1866→                signal.SIGHUP.
  1867→        """
  1868→        with tracer.start_as_current_span('pebble send_signal') as span:
  1869→            if isinstance(sig, int):
  1870→                sig = signal.Signals(sig).name
  1871→            span.set_attribute('signal', sig)
  1872→            payload = {
  1873→                'command': 'signal',
  1874→                'signal': {'name': sig},
  1875→            }
  1876→            msg = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True)
  1877→            self._control_ws.send(msg)
  1878→
  1879→
  1880→def _has_fileno(f: Any) -> bool:
  1881→    """Return ``True`` if the file-like object has a valid fileno() method."""
  1882→    try:
  1883→        f.fileno()
  1884→        return True
  1885→    except Exception:
  1886→        # Some types define a fileno method that raises io.UnsupportedOperation,
  1887→        # but just catching all exceptions here won't hurt.
  1888→        return False
  1889→

  1891→def _reader_to_websocket(
  1892→    reader: _WebsocketReader,
  1893→    ws: _WebSocket,
  1894→    encoding: str,
  1895→    cancel_reader: int | None = None,
  1896→    bufsize: int = 16 * 1024,
  1897→):
  1898→    """Read reader through to EOF and send each chunk read to the websocket."""
  1899→    while True:
  1900→        if cancel_reader is not None:
  1901→            # Wait for either a read to be ready or the caller to cancel stdin
  1902→            result = select.select([cancel_reader, reader], [], [])
  1903→            if cancel_reader in result[0]:
  1904→                break
  1905→
  1906→        chunk = reader.read(bufsize)
  1907→        if not chunk:
  1908→            break
  1909→        if isinstance(chunk, str):
  1910→            chunk = chunk.encode(encoding)
  1911→        ws.send_binary(chunk)
  1912→
  1913→    ws.send('{"command":"end"}')  # Send "end" command as TEXT frame to signal EOF
  1914→
  1915→
  1916→def _websocket_to_writer(ws: _WebSocket, writer: _WebsocketWriter, encoding: str | None):
  1917→    """Receive messages from websocket (until end signal) and write to writer."""
  1918→    while True:
  1919→        chunk = ws.recv()
  1920→
  1921→        if isinstance(chunk, str):
  1922→            try:
  1923→                payload = json.loads(chunk)
  1924→            except ValueError:
  1925→                # Garbage sent, try to keep going
  1926→                logger.warning('Cannot decode I/O command (invalid JSON)')
  1927→                continue
  1928→            command = payload.get('command')
  1929→            if command != 'end':
  1930→                # A command we don't recognize, keep going
  1931→                logger.warning('Invalid I/O command %r', command)
  1932→                continue
  1933→            # Received "end" command (EOF signal), stop thread
  1934→            break
  1935→
  1936→        if encoding is not None:
  1937→            chunk = chunk.decode(encoding)
  1938→        writer.write(chunk)
  1939→
  1940→
  1941→class _WebsocketWriter(io.BufferedIOBase):
  1942→    """A writable file-like object that sends what's written to it to a websocket."""
  1943→
  1944→    def __init__(self, ws: _WebSocket):
  1945→        self.ws = ws
  1946→
  1947→    def writable(self):
  1948→        """Denote this file-like object as writable."""
  1949→        return True
  1950→
  1951→    def write(self, chunk: str | bytes) -> int:
  1952→        """Write chunk to the websocket."""
  1953→        if not isinstance(chunk, bytes):
  1954→            raise TypeError(f'value to write must be bytes, not {type(chunk).__name__}')
  1955→        self.ws.send_binary(chunk)
  1956→        return len(chunk)
  1957→
  1958→    def close(self):
  1959→        """Send end-of-file message to websocket."""
  1960→        self.ws.send('{"command":"end"}')
  1961→
  1962→
  1963→class _WebsocketReader(io.BufferedIOBase):
  1964→    """A readable file-like object whose reads come from a websocket."""
  1965→
  1966→    def __init__(self, ws: _WebSocket):
  1967→        self.ws = ws
  1968→        self.remaining = b''
  1969→        self.eof = False
  1970→
  1971→    def readable(self) -> bool:
  1972→        """Denote this file-like object as readable."""
  1973→        return True
  1974→
  1975→    def read(self, n: int = -1) -> str | bytes:
  1976→        """Read up to n bytes from the websocket (or one message if n<0)."""
  1977→        if self.eof:
  1978→            # Calling read() multiple times after EOF should still return EOF
  1979→            return b''
  1980→
  1981→        while not self.remaining:
  1982→            chunk = self.ws.recv()
  1983→
  1984→            if isinstance(chunk, str):
  1985→                try:
  1986→                    payload = json.loads(chunk)
  1987→                except ValueError:
  1988→                    # Garbage sent, try to keep going
  1989→                    logger.warning('Cannot decode I/O command (invalid JSON)')
  1990→                    continue
  1991→                command = payload.get('command')
  1992→                if command != 'end':
  1993→                    # A command we don't recognize, keep going
  1994→                    logger.warning('Invalid I/O command %r', command)
  1995→                    continue
  1996→                # Received "end" command, return EOF designator
  1997→                self.eof = True
  1998→                return b''
  1999→
  2000→            self.remaining = chunk
  2001→
  2002→        if n < 0:
  2003→            n = len(self.remaining)
  2004→        result: str | bytes = self.remaining[:n]
  2005→        self.remaining = self.remaining[n:]
  2006→        return result
  2007→
  2008→    def read1(self, n: int = -1) -> str | bytes:
  2009→        """An alias for read."""
  2010→        return self.read(n)
  2011→

  2022→    def __str__(self) -> str:
  2023→        """Return the string value of the enum member as if it were really just a string.

  2025→        This aligns the behaviour of (str, enum.Enum) with Python 3.11's StrEnum.
  2026→        For example: str(IdentityAccess.ADMIN) -> 'admin'

  2028→        return str.__str__(self)

  2047→@dataclasses.dataclass
  2048→class BasicIdentity:
  2049→    """Basic identity configuration (for HTTP basic authentication)."""
  2050→
  2051→    password: str
  2052→    """sha512-crypt-hashed password.

  2055→    returned as ``*****``.

  2058→    """
  2059→

  2062→        """Create new BasicIdentity from dict parsed from JSON."""
  2063→        return cls(password=d['password'])
  2064→
  2065→    def to_dict(self) -> BasicIdentityDict:
  2066→        """Convert this basic identity to its dict representation."""
  2067→        return {'password': self.password}
  2068→
  2069→    def __str__(self) -> str:
  2070→        return f"{self.__class__.__name__}(password='*****')"

  2073→@dataclasses.dataclass
  2074→class Identity:
  2075→    """Pebble identity configuration."""
  2076→
  2077→    # NOTE: ensure <IdentityAccessLiterals> are kept up to date in all locations
  2078→    access: IdentityAccess | Literal['untrusted', 'metrics', 'read', 'admin']
  2079→    local: LocalIdentity | None = None
  2080→    basic: BasicIdentity | None = None
  2081→
  2082→    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
  2083→        """Validate that at least one of local or basic is provided."""
  2084→        if self.local is None and self.basic is None:
  2085→            raise ValueError('at least one of "local" or "basic" must be provided')
  2086→
  2087→    @classmethod
  2088→    def from_dict(cls, d: IdentityDict) -> Identity:
  2089→        """Create new Identity from dict parsed from JSON."""
  2090→        try:
  2091→            access = IdentityAccess(d['access'])
  2092→        except ValueError:
  2093→            # An unknown 'access' value, perhaps from a newer Pebble version
  2094→            # We silently preserve it for roundtrip safety
  2095→            access = d['access']
  2096→        local = LocalIdentity.from_dict(d['local']) if 'local' in d else None
  2097→        basic = BasicIdentity.from_dict(d['basic']) if 'basic' in d else None
  2098→        return cls(access=access, local=local, basic=basic)
  2099→
  2100→    def to_dict(self) -> IdentityDict:
  2101→        """Convert this identity to its dict representation."""
  2102→        result: IdentityDict = {
  2103→            'access': str(self.access)  # pyright: ignore[reportAssignmentType]
  2104→        }
  2105→        if self.local is not None:
  2106→            result['local'] = self.local.to_dict()
  2107→        if self.basic is not None:
  2108→            result['basic'] = self.basic.to_dict()
  2109→        return result
  2110→

  2112→class Client:
  2113→    """Pebble API client.
  2114→
  2115→    Defaults to using a Unix socket at socket_path (which must be specified
  2116→    unless a custom opener is provided).
  2117→
  2118→    For methods that wait for changes, such as :meth:`start_services` and :meth:`replan_services`,
  2119→    if the change fails or times out, then a :class:`ChangeError` or :class:`TimeoutError` will be
  2120→    raised.
  2121→
  2122→    All methods may raise exceptions when there are problems communicating with Pebble. Problems
  2123→    connecting to or transferring data with Pebble will raise a :class:`ConnectionError`. When an
  2124→    error occurs executing the request, such as trying to add an invalid layer or execute a command
  2125→    that does not exist, an :class:`APIError` is raised.
  2126→
  2127→    The ``timeout`` parameter specifies a timeout in seconds for blocking operations like the
  2128→    connection attempt to Pebble; used by ``urllib.request.OpenerDirector.open``. It's not for
  2129→    methods like :meth:`start_services` and :meth:`replan_services` mentioned above, and it's not
  2130→    for the command execution timeout defined in method :meth:`Client.exec`.
  2131→    """
  2132→
  2133→    _chunk_size = 8192
  2134→
  2135→    def __init__(
  2136→        self,
  2137→        socket_path: str,
  2138→        opener: urllib.request.OpenerDirector | None = None,
  2139→        base_url: str = 'http://localhost',
  2140→        timeout: float = 5.0,
  2141→    ):
  2142→        if not isinstance(socket_path, str):
  2143→            raise TypeError(f'`socket_path` should be a string, not: {type(socket_path)}')
  2144→        if opener is None:
  2145→            opener = self._get_default_opener(socket_path)
  2146→        self.socket_path = socket_path
  2147→        self.opener = opener
  2148→        self.base_url = base_url
  2149→        self.timeout = timeout
  2150→
  2151→    @classmethod
  2152→    def _get_default_opener(cls, socket_path: str) -> urllib.request.OpenerDirector:
  2153→        """Build the default opener to use for requests (HTTP over Unix socket)."""
  2154→        opener = urllib.request.OpenerDirector()
  2155→        opener.add_handler(_UnixSocketHandler(socket_path))
  2156→        opener.add_handler(urllib.request.HTTPDefaultErrorHandler())
  2157→        opener.add_handler(urllib.request.HTTPRedirectHandler())
  2158→        opener.add_handler(urllib.request.HTTPErrorProcessor())
  2159→        return opener
  2160→
  2161→    # we need to cast the return type depending on the request params
  2162→    def _request(
  2163→        self,
  2164→        method: str,
  2165→        path: str,
  2166→        query: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
  2167→        body: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
  2168→    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
  2169→        """Make a JSON request to the Pebble server with the given HTTP method and path.
  2170→
  2171→        If query dict is provided, it is encoded and appended as a query string
  2172→        to the URL. If body dict is provided, it is serialied as JSON and used
  2173→        as the HTTP body (with Content-Type: "application/json"). The resulting
  2174→        body is decoded from JSON.
  2175→        """
  2176→        headers = {'Accept': 'application/json'}
  2177→        data = None
  2178→        if body is not None:
  2179→            data = json.dumps(body).encode('utf-8')
  2180→            headers['Content-Type'] = 'application/json'
  2181→
  2182→        response = self._request_raw(method, path, query, headers, data)
  2183→        self._ensure_content_type(response.headers, 'application/json')
  2184→        raw_resp: dict[str, Any] = json.loads(response.read())
  2185→        return raw_resp
  2186→
  2187→    @staticmethod
  2188→    def _ensure_content_type(
  2189→        headers: email.message.Message,
  2190→        expected: Literal['multipart/form-data', 'application/json'],
  2191→    ):
  2192→        """Parse Content-Type header from headers and ensure it's equal to expected.
  2193→
  2194→        Return a dict of any options in the header, e.g., {'boundary': ...}.
  2195→        """
  2196→        ctype = headers.get_content_type()
  2197→        params = headers.get_params() or {}
  2198→        options = {key: value for key, value in params if value}
  2199→        if ctype != expected:

2200→            raise ProtocolError(f'expected Content-Type {expected!r}, got {ctype!r}')
  2201→        return options
  2202→
  2203→    def _request_raw(
  2204→        self,
  2205→        method: str,
  2206→        path: str,
  2207→        query: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
  2208→        headers: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
  2209→        data: bytes | Generator[bytes, Any, Any] | None = None,
  2210→    ) -> http.client.HTTPResponse:
  2211→        """Make a request to the Pebble server; return the raw HTTPResponse object."""
  2212→        url = self.base_url + path
  2213→        if query:
  2214→            url = f'{url}?{urllib.parse.urlencode(query, doseq=True)}'
  2215→
  2216→        if headers is None:
  2217→            headers = {}
  2218→        request = urllib.request.Request(url, method=method, data=data, headers=headers)  # noqa: S310
  2219→
  2220→        try:
  2221→            response = self.opener.open(request, timeout=self.timeout)
  2222→        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
  2223→            code = e.code
  2224→            status = e.reason
  2225→            try:
  2226→                body: dict[str, Any] = json.loads(e.read())
  2227→                message: str = body['result']['message']
  2228→            except (OSError, ValueError, KeyError) as e2:
  2229→                # Will only happen on read error or if Pebble sends invalid JSON.
  2230→                body: dict[str, Any] = {}
  2231→                message = f'{type(e2).__name__} - {e2}'
  2232→            raise APIError(body, code, status, message) from None
  2233→        except urllib.error.URLError as e:
  2234→            if e.args and isinstance(e.args[0], FileNotFoundError):
  2235→                raise ConnectionError(
  2236→                    f'Could not connect to Pebble: socket not found at {self.socket_path!r} '
  2237→                    '(container restarted?)'
  2238→                ) from None
  2239→            raise ConnectionError(e.reason) from e
  2240→
  2241→        return response
  2242→
  2243→    def get_system_info(self) -> SystemInfo:
  2244→        """Get system info."""
  2245→        with tracer.start_as_current_span('pebble get_system_info'):
  2246→            resp = self._request('GET', '/v1/system-info')
  2247→            return SystemInfo.from_dict(resp['result'])
  2248→
  2249→    def get_warnings(self, select: WarningState = WarningState.PENDING) -> list[Warning]:
  2250→        """Get list of warnings in given state (pending or all)."""
  2251→        with tracer.start_as_current_span('pebble get_warnings') as span:
  2252→            query = {'select': select.value}
  2253→            span.set_attributes(query)
  2254→            resp = self._request('GET', '/v1/warnings', query)
  2255→            return [Warning.from_dict(w) for w in resp['result']]
  2256→
  2257→    def ack_warnings(self, timestamp: datetime.datetime) -> int:
  2258→        """Acknowledge warnings up to given timestamp, return number acknowledged."""
  2259→        with tracer.start_as_current_span('pebble ack_warnings'):
  2260→            body = {'action': 'okay', 'timestamp': timestamp.isoformat()}
  2261→            resp = self._request('POST', '/v1/warnings', body=body)
  2262→            return resp['result']

  2264→    def get_changes(
  2265→        self,
  2266→        select: ChangeState = ChangeState.IN_PROGRESS,
  2267→        service: str | None = None,
  2268→    ) -> list[Change]:
  2269→        """Get list of changes in given state, filter by service name if given."""
  2270→        with tracer.start_as_current_span('pebble get_changes') as span:
  2271→            query: dict[str, str | int] = {'select': select.value}
  2272→            if service is not None:
  2273→                query['for'] = service
  2274→            span.set_attributes(query)
  2275→            resp = self._request('GET', '/v1/changes', query)
  2276→            return [Change.from_dict(c) for c in resp['result']]

  2284→    def abort_change(self, change_id: ChangeID) -> Change:

  2291→    def autostart_services(self, timeout: float = 30.0, delay: float = 0.1) -> ChangeID:

  2403→    def _services_action(
  2404→        self,
  2405→        action: str,
  2406→        services: Iterable[str],
  2407→        timeout: float | None,
  2408→        delay: float,
  2409→    ) -> ChangeID:
  2410→        with tracer.start_as_current_span(f'pebble {action}_services') as span:
  2411→            if isinstance(services, (str, bytes)) or not hasattr(services, '__iter__'):
  2412→                raise TypeError(
  2413→                    f'services must be of type Iterable[str], not {type(services).__name__}'
  2414→                )
  2415→
  2416→            services = list(services)
  2417→            for s in services:
  2418→                if not isinstance(s, str):
  2419→                    raise TypeError(f'service names must be str, not {type(s).__name__}')
  2420→
  2421→            body = {'action': action, 'services': services}
  2422→            span.set_attributes(body)
  2423→            resp = self._request('POST', '/v1/services', body=body)
  2424→            change_id = ChangeID(resp['change'])
  2425→            if timeout:
  2426→                change = self.wait_change(change_id, timeout=timeout, delay=delay)
  2427→                if change.err:
  2428→                    raise ChangeError(change.err, change)
  2429→            return change_id

  2431→    def wait_change(
  2432→        self,
  2433→        change_id: ChangeID,
  2434→        timeout: float | None = 30.0,
  2435→        delay: float = 0.1,
  2436→    ) -> Change:
  2437→        """Wait for the given change to be ready.
  2438→
  2439→        If the Pebble server supports the /v1/changes/{id}/wait API endpoint,
  2440→        use that to avoid polling, otherwise poll /v1/changes/{id} every delay
  2441→        seconds.
  2442→
  2443→        Args:
  2444→            change_id: Change ID of change to wait for.
  2445→            timeout: Maximum time in seconds to wait for the change to be
  2446→                ready. It may be ``None``, in which case wait_change never times out.
  2447→            delay: If polling, this is the delay in seconds between attempts.
  2448→
  2449→        Returns:
  2450→            The Change object being waited on.
  2451→
  2452→        Raises:
  2453→            TimeoutError: If the maximum timeout is reached.
  2454→        """
  2455→        with tracer.start_as_current_span('pebble wait_change'):
  2456→            try:
  2457→                return self._wait_change_using_wait(change_id, timeout)
  2458→            except NotImplementedError:
  2459→                # Pebble server doesn't support wait endpoint, fall back to polling
  2460→                return self._wait_change_using_polling(change_id, timeout, delay)
  2461→
  2462→    def _wait_change_using_wait(self, change_id: ChangeID, timeout: float | None):
  2463→        """Wait for a change to be ready using the wait-change API."""
  2464→        deadline = time.time() + timeout if timeout is not None else 0
  2465→
  2466→        # Hit the wait endpoint every Client.timeout-1 seconds to avoid long
  2467→        # requests (the -1 is to ensure it wakes up before the socket timeout)
  2468→        while True:
  2469→            this_timeout = max(self.timeout - 1, 1)  # minimum of 1 second
  2470→            if timeout is not None:
  2471→                time_remaining = deadline - time.time()
  2472→                if time_remaining <= 0:
  2473→                    break
  2474→                # Wait the lesser of the time remaining and Client.timeout-1
  2475→                this_timeout = min(time_remaining, this_timeout)
  2476→
  2477→            try:
  2478→                return self._wait_change(change_id, this_timeout)
  2479→            except builtins.TimeoutError:
  2480→                # Catch timeout from wait endpoint and loop to check deadline.
  2481→                pass
  2482→
  2483→        raise TimeoutError(f'timed out waiting for change {change_id} ({timeout} seconds)')
  2484→
  2485→    def _wait_change(self, change_id: ChangeID, timeout: float | None = None) -> Change:
  2486→        """Call the wait-change API endpoint directly."""
  2487→        query: dict[str, Any] = {}
  2488→        if timeout is not None:
  2489→            query['timeout'] = _format_timeout(timeout)
  2490→
  2491→        try:
  2492→            resp = self._request('GET', f'/v1/changes/{change_id}/wait', query)
  2493→        except APIError as e:
  2494→            if e.code == 404:
  2495→                raise NotImplementedError(
  2496→                    'server does not implement wait-change endpoint'
  2497→                ) from None
  2498→            if e.code == 504:
  2499→                raise TimeoutError(
  2500→                    f'timed out waiting for change {change_id} ({timeout} seconds)'
  2501→                ) from None
  2502→            raise
  2503→
  2504→        return Change.from_dict(resp['result'])
  2505→
  2506→    def _wait_change_using_polling(self, change_id: ChangeID, timeout: float | None, delay: float):
  2507→        """Wait for a change to be ready by polling the get-change API."""
  2508→        deadline = time.time() + timeout if timeout is not None else 0
  2509→
  2510→        while timeout is None or time.time() < deadline:
  2511→            change = self.get_change(change_id)
  2512→            if change.ready:
  2513→                return change
  2514→
  2515→            time.sleep(delay)
  2516→
  2517→        raise TimeoutError(f'timed out waiting for change {change_id} ({timeout} seconds)')

  2519→    def _checks_action(self, action: str, checks: Iterable[str]) -> list[str]:

  2521→            if isinstance(checks, str) or not hasattr(checks, '__iter__'):

  2523→                    f'checks must be of type Iterable[str], not {type(checks).__name__}',
  2524→                )
  2525→
  2526→            checks = tuple(checks)
  2527→            for chk in checks:
  2528→                if not isinstance(chk, str):
  2529→                    raise TypeError(f'check names must be str, not {type(chk).__name__}')

  2531→            span.set_attribute('checks', checks)

  2536→            # https://github.com/canonical/pebble/issues/788

  2539→    def add_layer(self, label: str, layer: str | LayerDict | Layer, *, combine: bool = False):
  2540→        """Dynamically add a new layer onto the Pebble configuration layers.
  2541→
  2542→        If combine is false (the default), append the new layer as the top
  2543→        layer with the given label. If combine is true and the label already
  2544→        exists, the two layers are combined into a single one considering the
  2545→        layer override rules; if the layer doesn't exist, it is added as usual.
  2546→        """
  2547→        with tracer.start_as_current_span('pebble add_layer') as span:
  2548→            if not isinstance(label, str):
  2549→                raise TypeError(f'label must be a str, not {type(label).__name__}')
  2550→            span.set_attribute('label', label)
  2551→            span.set_attribute('combine', combine)
  2552→
  2553→            if isinstance(layer, str):
  2554→                layer_yaml = layer
  2555→            elif isinstance(layer, dict):
  2556→                layer_yaml = Layer(layer).to_yaml()
  2557→            elif isinstance(layer, Layer):
  2558→                layer_yaml = layer.to_yaml()
  2559→            else:
  2560→                raise TypeError(
  2561→                    f'layer must be str, dict, or pebble.Layer, not {type(layer).__name__}'
  2562→                )
  2563→
  2564→            body = {
  2565→                'action': 'add',
  2566→                'combine': combine,
  2567→                'label': label,
  2568→                'format': 'yaml',
  2569→                'layer': layer_yaml,
  2570→            }
  2571→            self._request('POST', '/v1/layers', body=body)
  2572→
  2573→    def get_plan(self) -> Plan:
  2574→        """Get the Pebble plan (contains combined layer configuration)."""
  2575→        with tracer.start_as_current_span('pebble get_plan'):
  2576→            resp = self._request('GET', '/v1/plan', {'format': 'yaml'})
  2577→            return Plan(resp['result'])

  2579→    def get_services(self, names: Iterable[str] | None = None) -> list[ServiceInfo]:
  2580→        """Get the service status for the configured services.
  2581→
  2582→        If names is specified, only fetch the service status for the services
  2583→        named.

  2585→        with tracer.start_as_current_span('pebble get_services') as span:
  2586→            query = None

2600→    def pull(
  2601→        self, path: str | pathlib.PurePath, *, encoding: str | None = 'utf-8'
  2602→    ) -> BinaryIO | TextIO:
  2603→        """Read a file's content from the remote system.
  2604→
  2605→        Args:
  2606→            path: Path of the file to read from the remote system.
  2607→            encoding: Encoding to use for decoding the file's bytes to str,
  2608→                or ``None`` to specify no decoding.
  2609→
  2610→        Returns:
  2611→            A readable file-like object, whose read() method will return str
  2612→            objects decoded according to the specified encoding, or bytes if
  2613→            encoding is ``None``.
  2614→
  2615→        Raises:
  2616→            PathError: If there was an error reading the file at path, for
  2617→                example, if the file doesn't exist or is a directory.
  2618→        """
  2619→        path = str(path)
  2620→        with tracer.start_as_current_span('pebble pull') as span:
  2621→            query = {
  2622→                'action': 'read',
  2623→                'path': path,
  2624→            }
  2625→            span.set_attribute('path', path)
  2626→            headers = {'Accept': 'multipart/form-data'}
  2627→            response = self._request_raw('GET', '/v1/files', query, headers)
  2628→
  2629→            options = self._ensure_content_type(response.headers, 'multipart/form-data')
  2630→            boundary = options.get('boundary', '')
  2631→            if not boundary:
  2632→                raise ProtocolError(f'invalid boundary {boundary!r}')
  2633→
  2634→            parser = _FilesParser(boundary)
  2635→            try:
  2636→                while True:
  2637→                    chunk = response.read(self._chunk_size)
  2638→                    if not chunk:
  2639→                        break
  2640→                    parser.feed(chunk)
  2641→
  2642→                resp = parser.get_response()
  2643→                if resp is None:
  2644→                    raise ProtocolError('no "response" field in multipart body')
  2645→                self._raise_on_path_error(resp, path)
  2646→
  2647→                filenames = parser.filenames()
  2648→                if not filenames:
  2649→                    raise ProtocolError('no file content in multipart response')
  2650→                elif len(filenames) > 1:
  2651→                    raise ProtocolError('single file request resulted in a multi-file response')
  2652→
  2653→                filename = filenames[0]
  2654→                if filename != path:
  2655→                    raise ProtocolError(f'path not expected: {filename!r}')
  2656→                return parser.get_file(path, encoding)
  2657→            finally:
  2658→                parser.remove_files()
  2659→
  2660→    @staticmethod
  2661→    def _raise_on_path_error(resp: _FilesResponse, path: str):
  2662→        result = resp['result'] or []  # in case it's null instead of []
  2663→        paths = {item['path']: item for item in result}
  2664→        if path not in paths:
  2665→            raise ProtocolError(f'path not found in response metadata: {resp}')
  2666→        error = paths[path].get('error')
  2667→        if error:
  2668→            raise PathError(error['kind'], error['message'])
  2669→
  2670→    def push(
  2671→        self,
  2672→        path: str | pathlib.PurePath,
  2673→        source: _IOSource,
  2674→        *,
  2675→        encoding: str = 'utf-8',
  2676→        make_dirs: bool = False,
  2677→        permissions: int | None = None,
  2678→        user_id: int | None = None,
  2679→        user: str | None = None,
  2680→        group_id: int | None = None,
  2681→        group: str | None = None,
  2682→    ):
  2683→        """Write content to a given file path on the remote system.
  2684→
  2685→        Args:
  2686→            path: Path of the file to write to on the remote system.
  2687→            source: Source of data to write. This is either a concrete str or
  2688→                bytes instance, or a readable file-like object.
  2689→            encoding: Encoding to use for encoding source str to bytes, or
  2690→                strings read from source if it is a TextIO type. Ignored if
  2691→                source is bytes or BinaryIO.
  2692→            make_dirs: If true, create parent directories if they don't exist.
  2693→            permissions: Permissions (mode) to create file with (Pebble default
  2694→                is 0o644).
  2695→            user_id: User ID (UID) for file. If neither ``group_id`` nor ``group`` is provided,
  2696→                the group is set to the user's default group.
  2697→            user: Username for file. User's UID must match ``user_id`` if both are
  2698→                specified. If neither ``group_id`` nor ``group`` is provided,
  2699→                the group is set to the user's default group.
  2700→            group_id: Group ID (GID) for file. May only be specified with ``user_id`` or ``user``.
  2701→            group: Group name for file. Group's GID must match ``group_id`` if
  2702→                both are specified. May only be specified with ``user_id`` or ``user``.
  2703→
  2704→        Raises:
  2705→            PathError: If there was an error writing the file to the path; for example, if the
  2706→                destination path doesn't exist and ``make_dirs`` is not used.
  2707→        """
  2708→        path = str(path)
  2709→        with tracer.start_as_current_span('pebble push') as span:
  2710→            info = self._make_auth_dict(permissions, user_id, user, group_id, group)
  2711→            info['path'] = path
  2712→            if make_dirs:
  2713→                info['make-dirs'] = True
  2714→            span.set_attributes(info)  # type: ignore
  2715→            metadata = {
  2716→                'action': 'write',
  2717→                'files': [info],
  2718→            }
  2719→
  2720→            data, content_type = self._encode_multipart(metadata, path, source, encoding)
  2721→
  2722→            headers = {
  2723→                'Accept': 'application/json',
  2724→                'Content-Type': content_type,
  2725→            }
  2726→            response = self._request_raw('POST', '/v1/files', None, headers, data)
  2727→            self._ensure_content_type(response.headers, 'application/json')
  2728→            resp = json.loads(response.read())
  2729→            # we need to cast the Dict[Any, Any] to _FilesResponse
  2730→            self._raise_on_path_error(typing.cast('_FilesResponse', resp), path)
  2731→
  2732→    @staticmethod
  2733→    def _make_auth_dict(
  2734→        permissions: int | None,
  2735→        user_id: int | None,
  2736→        user: str | None,
  2737→        group_id: int | None,
  2738→        group: str | None,
  2739→    ) -> _AuthDict:
  2740→        d: _AuthDict = {}
  2741→        if permissions is not None:
  2742→            d['permissions'] = format(permissions, '03o')
  2743→        if user_id is not None:
  2744→            d['user-id'] = user_id
  2745→        if user is not None:
  2746→            d['user'] = user
  2747→        if group_id is not None:
  2748→            d['group-id'] = group_id
  2749→        if group is not None:
  2750→            d['group'] = group
  2751→        return d
  2752→
  2753→    def _encode_multipart(
  2754→        self, metadata: dict[str, Any], path: str, source: _IOSource, encoding: str
  2755→    ):
  2756→        # Python's stdlib mime/multipart handling is screwy and doesn't handle
  2757→        # binary properly, so roll our own.
  2758→        if isinstance(source, str):
  2759→            source_io: _AnyStrFileLikeIO = io.StringIO(source)
  2760→        elif isinstance(source, bytes):
  2761→            source_io: _AnyStrFileLikeIO = io.BytesIO(source)
  2762→        else:
  2763→            source_io: _AnyStrFileLikeIO = source
  2764→        boundary = binascii.hexlify(os.urandom(16))
  2765→        path_escaped = path.replace('"', '\\"').encode('utf-8')
  2766→        content_type = f'multipart/form-data; boundary="{boundary.decode("utf-8")}"'
  2767→
  2768→        def generator() -> Generator[bytes, None, None]:
  2769→            yield b''.join([
  2770→                b'--',
  2771→                boundary,
  2772→                b'\r\n',
  2773→                b'Content-Type: application/json\r\n',
  2774→                b'Content-Disposition: form-data; name="request"\r\n',
  2775→                b'\r\n',
  2776→                json.dumps(metadata).encode('utf-8'),
  2777→                b'\r\n',
  2778→                b'--',
  2779→                boundary,
  2780→                b'\r\n',
  2781→                b'Content-Type: application/octet-stream\r\n',
  2782→                b'Content-Disposition: form-data; name="files"; filename="',
  2783→                path_escaped,
  2784→                b'"\r\n',
  2785→                b'\r\n',
  2786→            ])
  2787→
  2788→            content: str | bytes = source_io.read(self._chunk_size)
  2789→            while content:
  2790→                if isinstance(content, str):
  2791→                    content = content.encode(encoding)
  2792→                yield content
  2793→                content = source_io.read(self._chunk_size)
  2794→
  2795→            yield b''.join([
  2796→                b'\r\n',
  2797→                b'--',
  2798→                boundary,
  2799→                b'--\r\n',
  2800→            ])
  2801→
  2802→        return generator(), content_type
  2803→
  2804→    def list_files(
  2805→        self, path: str | pathlib.PurePath, *, pattern: str | None = None, itself: bool = False
  2806→    ) -> list[FileInfo]:
  2807→        """Return list of directory entries from given path on remote system.
  2808→
  2809→        Despite the name, this method returns a list of files *and*
  2810→        directories, similar to :func:`os.listdir` or :func:`os.scandir`.
  2811→
  2812→        Args:
  2813→            path: Path of the directory to list, or path of the file to return
  2814→                information about.
  2815→            pattern: If specified, filter the list to just the files that match,
  2816→                for example ``*.txt``.
  2817→            itself: If path refers to a directory, return information about the
  2818→                directory itself, rather than its contents.
  2819→
  2820→        Raises:
  2821→            PathError: if there was an error listing the directory; for example, if the directory
  2822→                does not exist.
  2823→        """
  2824→        path = str(path)
  2825→        with tracer.start_as_current_span('pebble list_files') as span:
  2826→            query = {'path': path}
  2827→            if pattern:
  2828→                query['pattern'] = pattern
  2829→            if itself:
  2830→                query['itself'] = 'true'
  2831→            span.set_attributes(query)
  2832→            query['action'] = 'list'
  2833→            resp = self._request('GET', '/v1/files', query)
  2834→            result: list[_FileInfoDict] = resp['result'] or []  # in case it's null instead of []
  2835→            return [FileInfo.from_dict(d) for d in result]
  2836→
  2837→    def make_dir(
  2838→        self,
  2839→        path: str | pathlib.PurePath,
  2840→        *,
  2841→        make_parents: bool = False,
  2842→        permissions: int | None = None,
  2843→        user_id: int | None = None,
  2844→        user: str | None = None,
  2845→        group_id: int | None = None,
  2846→        group: str | None = None,
  2847→    ):
  2848→        """Create a directory on the remote system with the given attributes.
  2849→
  2850→        Args:
  2851→            path: Path of the directory to create on the remote system.
  2852→            make_parents: If true, create parent directories if they don't exist.
  2853→            permissions: Permissions (mode) to create directory with (Pebble
  2854→                default is 0o755).
  2855→            user_id: User ID (UID) for directory. If neither ``group_id`` nor ``group``
  2856→                is provided, the group is set to the user's default group.
  2857→            user: Username for directory. User's UID must match ``user_id`` if
  2858→                both are specified. If neither ``group_id`` nor ``group`` is provided,
  2859→                the group is set to the user's default group.
  2860→            group_id: Group ID (GID) for directory.
  2861→                May only be specified with ``user_id`` or ``user``.
  2862→            group: Group name for directory. Group's GID must match ``group_id``
  2863→                if both are specified. May only be specified with ``user_id`` or ``user``.
  2864→
  2865→        Raises:
  2866→            PathError: if there was an error making the directory; for example, if the parent path
  2867→                does not exist, and ``make_parents`` is not used.
  2868→        """
  2869→        path = str(path)
  2870→        with tracer.start_as_current_span('pebble make_dir') as span:
  2871→            info = self._make_auth_dict(permissions, user_id, user, group_id, group)
  2872→            info['path'] = path
  2873→            if make_parents:
  2874→                info['make-parents'] = True
  2875→            span.set_attributes(info)  # type: ignore
  2876→            body = {
  2877→                'action': 'make-dirs',
  2878→                'dirs': [info],
  2879→            }
  2880→            resp = self._request('POST', '/v1/files', None, body)
  2881→            self._raise_on_path_error(typing.cast('_FilesResponse', resp), path)
  2882→
  2883→    def remove_path(self, path: str | pathlib.PurePath, *, recursive: bool = False):
  2884→        """Remove a file or directory on the remote system.
  2885→
  2886→        Args:
  2887→            path: Path of the file or directory to delete from the remote system.
  2888→            recursive: If true, and path is a directory, recursively delete it and
  2889→                       everything under it. If path is a file, delete the file. In
  2890→                       either case, do nothing if the file or directory does not
  2891→                       exist. Behaviourally similar to ``rm -rf <file|dir>``.
  2892→
  2893→        Raises:
  2894→            pebble.PathError: If a relative path is provided, or if ``recursive`` is ``False``
  2895→                and the file or directory cannot be removed (it does not exist or is not empty).
  2896→        """
  2897→        path = str(path)
  2898→        with tracer.start_as_current_span('pebble remove_path') as span:
  2899→            info: dict[str, Any] = {'path': path}
  2900→            if recursive:
  2901→                info['recursive'] = True
  2902→            span.set_attributes(info)
  2903→            body = {
  2904→                'action': 'remove',
  2905→                'paths': [info],
  2906→            }
  2907→            resp = self._request('POST', '/v1/files', None, body)
  2908→            self._raise_on_path_error(typing.cast('_FilesResponse', resp), path)
  2909→
  2910→    # Exec I/O is str if encoding is provided (the default)
  2911→    @typing.overload

  2913→        self,

  2918→        working_dir: str | pathlib.PurePath | None = None,
